text: Overall I’m pretty happy with this purchase bc the ink is good & I’ve purchased it before.  The green is gorgeous.  The purple didn’t really wow me, but it flows nicely. The blue however is a disappointment bc it’s extremely dry & keeps drying in my pen. I am using the inks in the same type of pen- platinum curidas & all have same size nib. So the only difference is the actual ink.  I’m gonna try to mix a little of the ink with a tiny bit of glycerin next time I refill it & see if that helps it to stay wetter & not dry out in my pen. Aside from that I love their ink. Think I just received a dud this time with the blue.
product: Texas Instruments TI-84 Plus Graphing Calculator

text:
[[VIDEOID:63276c19932aa4f3687042b8b9f8613c]] Update: tried again to return them. I have prime. Amazon wants $7.99 to pick them up. Why? Bc I return garbage products & I don’t feel like I should be penalized when third party sellers & amazon send damaged/defective products & then expect me to be happy with them & keep them. Why should I bear the burden of a billion dollar plus corporation as a disabled veteran living on disability? These skip all over the place. The yellow won’t write no matter how much I shake the ball bearing. It and the black give a second of ink & stop. I even tried warming the ink up. The black finally became more viscous, but the yellow won’t lay down. If you have a fast hand & are used to taking fast notes these are useless. At least my set is. Garbage. Utter & complete garbage. Some work better if you write slow like a toddler, but I have never had to slow my hand with a zebra fountain pen nor any of the 200 others I own. And you don’t have to shake the zebra pen!

I purchased these bc of the reviews. Maybe I receive a bad batch. Idk. Mine are awful. I have fountain pens I purchased online for a dollar and less that works better & that write every single time I pick them up. Half of these did not even work after I attached the ink. I had to squeeze the cartridge repeatedly & even then I’m lucky if I get anything to come out. For $18 I was expecting to at least get something that would write every time I picked it up. These are a scratchy mess. I have tried them on cheap paper & expensive Tomoe River paper with the same results. I have hundreds of fountain pens, but I was looking for something that I could visually see at a moment’s glance what color was in the pen & I thought these would work. I will give them a few more days, but if the ink doesn’t settle in & lubricate the innards so that the writing experience is improved then I will definitely be sending these back bc they are garbage as-is.
product: Texas Instruments TI-84 Plus Graphing Calculator

text: It’s a beautiful color, but even though it had been packed extremely well imo it still arrived & showed considerable loss of ink due to shipping/handling disturbances.  It also left a stain on my bathroom vanity that I am still working on & thankfully it’s just a small one as I placed paper towels down before I opened the mess.  Next time I receive a bottle like this I’m just going to rinse it off, pat it dry, & slip a non-latex glove over it to keep everything in the vicinity safe including my hands bc the ink stained the label & wont come off except if my hands happen to be wet/moist.  The ink works well on both Tomoe River paper & cheap notebook paper & dries quickly.  As for the ink itself: I love it!  I don’t understand why it stained my vanity tho bc to my knowledge it’s not supposed to be waterproof.  Luckily after much scrubbing there is only a tiny mark left & I know it’s there, but I am really hoping my LL does not notice it.  Gonna try a little bleach on it next.  Also, I was able to mix a bit of it with a J Herbin red ink (scented red rose I believe) that I had on hand to get another shade of purple & I am quite pleased with it.  It is currently in a Dryden pen rn & it’s working beautifully with zero issues. This ink R&K is in a Lamy  “Candy” purple pen & is also working beautifully.  I wish they offered the inks in glass bottles instead of the cheap plastic.  Tho I recently received a bottle of Pilot Iroshiziku ink with a cracked lid & zero ink as it had obviously been upside down at some point during transit so glass is no guarantee that ink doesn’t get damaged. I’m disabled so I generally order everything online as it’s easier for me, but it appears I am going to have to venture out of my cave at some point. Lol. The ink is lovely if you can get one that has not been damaged or lost too much of it’s contents en route as I have.  I did notice that the cap on mine was dented & I believe that’s probably what allowed the ink to escape as they always tell us not to buy dented cans at grocery stores!  Things are only as good as their weakest links.
product: Texas Instruments TI-84 Plus Graphing Calculator

text: Idk if I just got a bad batch which is possible I suppose bc let’s face it sometimes companies have bad manufacturing runs, but all 4 of my pens are a total pain to use. They skip or the ink just does not flow at all no matter how hard I press on the Nov & then I get a puddle of ink.  Every once in a blue moon if I dip them in ink they seem to work a bit better, but it’s definitely not how they were meant to be used. I am not a newbie either. I own well over 200 fountain pens & calligraphy pens of versing widths & I have a large collection of inks as well. I also tried these out on every type of paper I have from Tomoe River to Mead & composition notebooks, but the pens I have are just useless for me as they will not write fluidly.  If I wanted to stop & start a hundred x I would just stick with my dip pens.  These are a huge let down as they have so many high reviews & I just wasn’t expecting them to be so awful.  Idk if I’m going to try the amazon replacement or not.  In my experience I tend to get another item from the same bad quality lot (or worse).  Decisions, decisions.
product: Texas Instruments TI-84 Plus Graphing Calculator

text: Great paper. I own others by this brand. This is going back for a replacement tho bc it arrived damaged. Both the front & back covers are damaged.  Even the paper on the inside shows dents from whatever damaged the front & back covers. It has creases along the bottoms of about half the pages about 1” from the right edge.
product: Texas Instruments TI-84 Plus Graphing Calculator

text:
[[VIDEOID:ffd2a834f42a38fd089e2bfa848e4b5d]] Idk what to say bc what I received looks nothing like the one advertised. What I received looks like someone poured motor oil on leather after they stamped it then went on a long lunch & came back & tried to fix the mess they left on the leather. It looks like they tried to distress or something which I hate to begin with as I’m not a fan of fake distressing. I find that seems to be the norm when the leather is not the best quality. When the leather is thinner or more scratched up as this is the sellers seem to think it’s best to distress it.

The stamping itself is well done. The only problem with it is that the leather is so thin & thinner even in some places that it has created humps in the cover front & back. I am not joking about the motor oil. It feels & smells like motor oil. Their is a slick substance on my hands after touching it & I am allergic to petroleum products so I’ve had to wash my hands several times & put on gloves. The distressing job was so uneven that if I decide to keep the journal I will definitely not keep it in this incarnation. I will definitely use leather cleaner to remove whatever I can sue to my allergies & then I will have to cover it with something else that I’m not allergic to, but that just adds time & money to this so idk what I’m going to do yet. I’ve even thought of painting it & sealing it. Fortunately the dye job on the front isn’t as bad as the one on the back & I’m the only one that will see it as it’s not a gift for anyone except myself.

I like the locking mechanism & feel they did a good job on thst tho it was covered in the oil too. That has made it easy to open tho. Lol. However, bc of the locking mechanism this is not a journal you will be able to paste or collage many things into. It’s a straight up writing journal imo bc it’s in no way expandable due to the locking device. Tho I suppose if your crafty/handy you can change out the rivets in the back & add a longer piece of leather there after you have filled it to ur heart’s desire.

As for the paper I don’t find it any better than any paper I’ve had in similar journals. It has flecks & bits of other papers which I personally don’t care for bc I use fountain pens & dip pens & my pens will skip on the uneven surfaces & that is literally on every page. There are also quite a few wrinkled pages in the journal which you can find with homemade journals.

So at this point I just don’t know. I need to think about it a bit more. If I had received a journal that looked as good as the advertised one & was not covered in oil & I would definitely keep it & just use different pens or use it for watercolor or bird sketches. This one requires more thought. I definitely would not give this as a gift to anyone unless I ordered it to be delivered to me & had a chance to clean it up & check it out first, but if you are a crafty person who likes a challenge or is willing to take a chance & hope for one like the pic then idk maybe take a chance?
product: Texas Instruments TI-84 Plus Graphing Calculator

text: Literally one of the worst packing jobs I’ve seen.  It’s the lightest pen I have ever used & as I am post surgery on my dominant hand & am used to less than 5 minutes of writing even remotely comfortably I absolutely love that part.  I think it’s pricey for what it is though. The Pilot Petit are $3 on Jet pens. 8 x cheaper & imho write just as well. Where this has the advantage for me is tho I am a woman I am tall & have man hands so said two hubbies. Bloody mean things to say if you ask me. Lol.  I am in love with it even though I hate the price point & truly believe it to be massively overpriced for plastic, but being s fountain pen junkie with well over a hundred including Montblanc I fully intend to buy bc thst is what we fountain pen ppl do. We collect them in all shapes & sizes. I have 5 kids & I tend to collect them in both my & my children’s & my grandchildren’s favorite colors.  Some I gift while I am alive & others they will only inherit bc I use them daily for writing & art.  It is a little beauty & I love that the cap screws on.  This mint color is beautiful & just happens to match a cutting mat I picked up at Wally World recently. Lol. Yay me!
product: Texas Instruments TI-84 Plus Graphing Calculator

text: [[VIDEOID:c4ffdd909a55f78e5a83951e34c4528e]] Unfortunately I received a pen that has some very bad metal parts that has the actual gold finish missing in quite a few places & is scratched up in many others.  That means the metal parts actually test my skin & cut it because the metal is actual quite jagged in many places. I don’t know if the metal pieces were dropped or were caught in some machinery or what, but it makes for a very unpleasant feeling when you hold it & a very unsafe result when it cut my fingers in several places.  I liked the look of the ribbed design of the metal on the pen, but it made for a less than ideal experience when actually holding the pen as it was uncomfortable.  It’s a very pretty pen & I found it quite beautiful even though it honestly triggered issues with my vertigo. Something about the design where it looks almost holographic & like the design is actually moving kicked my vertigo into high gear which is unfortunate bc the design was the entire reason I purchased the pen in the first place. Lol.  The issue with the workmanship should be able to ne seen in the pics as there are quite a few globs of resin or acrylic down the side of the pen which are a darker yellow stain than the rest of the pen & there are globs of what must be wood pulp or shavings in the resin that end up sitting directly on top of the top layer & you can both see them & feel them when you run your hand over the pen. Which is extremely annoying to someone who likes to pet her fountain pens.  It would definitely be worth checking back in a couple of months as the crafter gets better at their trade and/or uses better materials.  However, right now with these damaged metal materials with nicks, scratches, & paint missing & with the flaws/damages to the wood I’m not in the mood for a replacement & I intend to go for a return/refund.  Though I do hope to try this seller again at a later date if I see an improvement in the issues bc I prefer hand made fountain pens.
product: Texas Instruments TI-84 Plus Graphing Calculator

text: [[VIDEOID:b5de3fbb67572893aa86f7052023b9ba]] I’m dumbfounded by the great reviews. Maybe I just received a dud? Idk.  My pen leaks like a sieve as you can see in the pics & by the ink collected on my fingers. The cap will not post which makes me crazy.  The clip is askew. It’s like a third grader put it together with left over fittings & tried to make it fit. The fittings are a hot mess too. The gold is all scratched, dinged, & dented & it came like that right out of the packaging.  I also can’t u deer and why they have 2 shades of teal making up the pen body. The very tip is one shader & it just looks like they ran out of resin imo.  The resin itself has some obvious impurities in it. Idk if it was dusty where these were made or what, but something definitely got into the mix that produced this pen.  I am a fountain pen freak & I have purchased pens off eBay for 99 cents or less that write 100x better than this pen. The nib is so scratchy it has actually torn paper & I have a very light hand & use pricey papers.  I love Jane Davenport stuff, but after seeing the cancer warnings no thx.  I’ll purchase from artists who care as much about my health as I do.
product: Texas Instruments TI-84 Plus Graphing Calculator

text: I’ve used Carter’s for 4 decades in a work environment & home environment for arts & crafts, but I have never in my life received one looking like this. Looks like some kid stick their fingers in it.  Lol. And the seal actually looked as tho it had been previously compromised when I went to open it. I wish I had thought to take a pic of that.  I’m less than thrilled bc I needed this asap for a current project.  The front of the pad is pretty beat up too.  It has a very obvious dent in it which I would assume is when the interior pad got messed up making it hard to get stamps to pick up the ink evenly without applying a lot of pressure & then you get parts of the stamp that aren’t part of the design. This seems like a scratch & dent sale ink pad to me which sucks bc I love Carter’s & have used them for decades as I stated before.  The pad appears to be inked well & is not dry. It even seems to close pretty well considering the huge dent in the lid, but it does not work for the purposes I purchased it for. I’m hoping I can return it or exchange it.
product: Texas Instruments TI-84 Plus Graphing Calculator

text: New to this brand & as a huge fan & collector of fountain pens I am now a huge fan of this brand.  I purchased the 5-1/2” x 8” size & a smaller one to throw in my purse.  I plan on purchasing more in the future as my fountain pens have finally found a home for their ink & words.  I am very excited about the two ribbons too.  The only issue I see that already annoys me immensely as these are brand spanking new right out of the plastic is that they are missing the green color in several places. Idk if it has flaked off or what, but these were sealed in plastic when I I received them. I have handled them exactly  one time since opening them so idk how that paint job on the cover is going to hold up to daily use.  I knocked of a star for that bc imo they should not be flaking off their color after one handling of extremely light use.  I’m disabled with a bad arm so I’m not able to be rough with it even if I wanted to. Lol. It’s lost the green color at the top & the bottom already.  That is concerning to me as it’s not even been placed on a book shelf yet, but just been lying on a table tray by itself.
product: Texas Instruments TI-84 Plus Graphing Calculator

text:
Update: received replacement & the second is just like the first unfortunately. So I will assume this was made by the same crafter & from the same batch as the last. Again, the paper has been pulled too tightly & ripped through the backing. It has also been cut through the first 30% of the pages at one edge. There are paint pen marking all over the journal inside & out. This one has a cut down the front where maybe they had begun to make a scoring cut in the leather & realized it was going to be too short & then just put the design over it. They did a better job of getting a deeper embossing on this one tho. The finish is worse however. This one has a weird finish almost like pumice stones where it looks kind of crackled & porous Idk if it was done on purpose as a design or not. I wish this crafter would learn to not use paint pens to mark up leather bc it ruins the product. If you try to remove the paint pen you ruin the leather. I am double bummed for having given them a second chance.
Old review:
This could have been a beautiful little hotter notebook. Unfortunately it is not. The ppl that made it decided to use metallic paint pens & permanent markers to mark all their sewing lines ruining the aesthetic. There is not a spot on either side without a blemish. I had resolved myself that if only the inside was ugly I could live with that. Lol. Bc there’s a lot of that irl imo, but it turned out that both the inside & outside had imperfections behind what I was prepared to pay for & live with. As a disabled person I can generally live with a lot lol, but I spend my little bit of money on writing tools & I like them to be nice. This could have been a thing of true beauty as it reminds me of several Levenger notebooks I own. Sadly, while the idea was great the execution is lacking. I hope they will do it right next time bc it’s the perfect size to throw into a purse or backpack for a short trip or even a longer jaunt if you bring paper refills. The paper quality was not what I was hoping for either & as it arrived torn & already pulling away from its base I would not have used it anyways. As it is it is a hard no for me in its current state. I hope that either the seller or amazon will do right me. Someone should definitely be doing quality inspection checks bc this would not have passed even a rudimentary one. The metallic silver paint pen was a glaring no staring me right in the face the second I opened it up. The black permanent marker while it did match the journal better was still extremely obvious & still extremely annoying to see. It’s a well thought out design in my estimation just an extremely flawed execution. I hope the seller tweaks their issues & offers it in other colors bc I would totally try again.
product: Texas Instruments TI-84 Plus Graphing Calculator

text: It’s a nice heavy journal with thick pages.  The rose gold color is very pretty. Of course the first thing I noticed when I pulled it from the package was that the upper right hand corner had already been bent.  For some reason the quick reference page has been glued in weirdly imo so that there is no margin to the left & it makes it hard to write ur information there.  I haven’t written mine in there yet bc my entire book seems to have toner or printer ink or something on every single page like someone decided to just sprinkle some fairy dust on every page.  As I intend to use this partly as an art journal I would prefer one without the added splotches all over.  I believe there are 43 sheets of lined paper after the journal section & I am quite happy about that added bonus.  The envelope in the back is quite roomy & will hold a lot of receipts.  It’s a pretty journal & I suspect I will buy more from the manufacturer in the future albeit hopefully without these issues bc this one needs to be replaced & hopefully Amazon will do so.
product: Texas Instruments TI-84 Plus Graphing Calculator

text: My review is whacked bc I received the wrong pens & I am extremely unhappy about it. I have not had a very good week with Amazon in particular.  I love Amazon, but I keep receiving packages that are damaged containing items that are damaged.  Now this mess arrives. Argh!  The pens are excellent in every regard. They just aren’t the colors ordered. I wanted the purple & yellow pens & as you can see they are not in my order.  I ordered a journal earlier this week that came with some pens & stencils & the pens are too thick to fit in the stencils. These do an amazing job in them. The stencils have very tight corners & are very sharp & these handle them beautifully. I just did not want to waste ink filling them in. Lol. The first thing I noticed about these pens was the heft. I love a pen with some heft to it. Even tho I’ve had hand surgery & I can no longer write, paint, or draw as long as I used to I have always loved the feel of a well balanced writing instrument in my hand. These aren’t exactly balanced lol bc of the ink distribution & the plastic fatigue finger rest, but it feels nice in my hand.  I’m just thoroughly ticked off that these are not what I paid for & what I have been waiting for. Lol.  Back to my pen search.
product: Texas Instruments TI-84 Plus Graphing Calculator

text: It’s pretty, but it’s not very sturdy. Mine arrived looking like it had been dropped on its edges either before or during transit tho I suppose it could have happened in a warehouse somewhere.  Even if I was willing to overlook that- which I am most definitely not- I opened it up to see that the tabbed monthly pages were all bent and folded too.  They are just regular paper pages with a little tan at the top that has a protective tape over it to make it sturdier i guess. Clearly it does not make it stuffy enough to survive the shipping.  I briefly thought that I would keep it & try to make it work bc returns are such a hassle for me bc I am disabled.  I get really annoyed with having to watch out for porch pirates trying to steal my returns if I put them on my porch to wait for UPS.  I am both physically disabled & hearing impaired so I have 2 service dogs, but I have found that if I try to keep my returns inside my home until UPS arrives they either don’t ring my doorbell to alert my service dogs who then alert me or they leave before I can physically get myself to the door. So yeah- returns are a big deal to me. Lol.  Bc if miss the window for UPS to pick it up (they only try once now) I then have to take it to a drop off center. Did I mention I am disabled? Lol. And I don’t drive?  Yeah. Big time fun at my house with returns.  So then I would have to pay more for a taxi there & back than what the products are worth. Lol. Not to mention my pain & suffering throughout all of it.  I just can’t help feeling that sometimes stuff gets shipped that has already been dropped or dinged bc I have received an awful lot lately  & it’s really ticking me off.  If I wanted dropped & dinged stuff I would buy used not new!  Hence the very long winded diatribe of venting here.  Let’s see if Amazon will post it. Lol.
product: Texas Instruments TI-84 Plus Graphing Calculator

text: I’m a book freak & I adore mine!  I use fountain pens. Cheap ones off eBay I paid anywhere from 5 cents to 99 cents for up to my Montblanc pens & Pilot pens & they all work fine. Sure you may have a little paper on the end of ur nib once in a while, but to me it’s nbd bc it homemade paper & I think they did a marvelous job on it. I’m not generally a fan of the “antiquing” of anything. I usually hate it, but I fell in love w the silver paper in this journal & decided why not?  The only thing I would change is the closure. I hate the tab thingy. I prefer the wrap around string method to close my journals or anything that does not leave me any type of uneven surface to write on later.  It’s a neat design feature to some ppl & it does look good & cute, but it’s not my personal preference.  I would not let that keep me from buying another one though. Lol. I do hope they make them with a different closing option in the future though bc I love the size of the book & the superior craftsmanship.  It’s well worth the price!
product: Texas Instruments TI-84 Plus Graphing Calculator

text: It’s beautiful. I wasn’t sure if I would be happy with it given the price I paid which I felt was costly, but I’m happy enough with it. Bought it to record bird sightings in my backyard. I’m disabled & I am trying to find new ways to keep my mind off chronic pain which can consume a lot of brain cells.  Sadly, I took a disastrous fall shortly after buying it bc my neighbor blamed my bird feeders for injuring a local squirrel that likes to raid my bird feeders even though I left food for the squirrels too. The exact same food. I kid you not, but the evil little bigger decided to get on a light branch I was advised to place the feeder on by a bird conservancy place that “should keep the squirrels off of it”. Ha!  The squirrel was limping from a fall. I went out & stood on a 36” crate & took a nasty fall & I now have to have another back surgery. Proof that no good deed goes unpunished as the saying goes. Lol.  The fall lead to a concussion & repeated blackouts & exacerbated sciatica & massive swelling on my head, face, butt, elbows, hips, knees, etc. lol. Literally everywhere that hit the ground when I fell. So the irony is I have not even been able to use my beautiful bird journal to record any bird sightings since I bought it bc I am laid up awaiting back surgery. The last one left me disabled 15 years ago. Anyone reading this please keep me (Sherri) & my family in ur prayers bc if my past surgery is anything to go by i & we will need it.  So… now that you know my life story & I have saved $500 on therapy… buy the journal bc it’s simply lovely. Lol.
product: Texas Instruments TI-84 Plus Graphing Calculator

text: It’s pretty. The color is gorgeous. It has nice paper. I can use my fountain pens in it even the cheapest 99 cent ones I buy on eBay. Lol. What’s not to love?  But it bc you will not regret it. And truth bomb: I bought this for my grandson - and kept it! Lol. Yeah- I’m that b*tch!,  but I gave him something better in his opinion as a kid. Win-win!
product: Texas Instruments TI-84 Plus Graphing Calculator

text: Where has this been since I became disabled & mostly bedridden?  I’ve been through so many subpar bed trays it’s ridiculous. This one is a gem!  I absolutely love it!  I am stunned that my art easel actually fits it & even better two of the plastic legs nicely fit into the book ledge groove to keep it nice & steady while I draw & paint.  I love the ski sled legs because it makes it so easy to move when I have to get up. One of the previous types I tried was so heavy I could barely lift it due to my disabilities, but this is sturdy & light.  The only thing I might do is add 2 pieces of small rubber to the ski sled legs at the ends by my body for added stability so that it doesn’t move when I don’t want it to because I suffer with vertigo & sleep apnea & I don’t want it to move if I fall over. Lol. So I would still be able to just lightly lift the legs w the rubber attached & still be able to push it forward easily enough. The only other thing I miss & need is a cup holder as I am on medications & need to stay hydrated & do lots of arts & crafts where I need to have water handy. I understand they probably didn’t add it bc most ppl buy it to use as a laptop table & electronics & fluids are never a hood idea, but I wish I had the option to choose for myself. I’ve thought about drilling a hole in the top & adding a cup holder or more likely just adding one to the side. As I will likely add some cup holders for my pens, pencils, & brushes, & craft supplies. I also wish that they offered an even larger size.  For those of us who are stuck in our beds & literally live in them there are very few options that are not homemade.  I would love one to go the full length of my queen bed & have designed several of them. Lol. As I live alone with 2 service dogs there’s nobody to complain about me dropping beads in the bed or spiking paint on the sheets. Lol.  To the manufacturers: bravo on a well-designed product & please for the love of God & this disabled woman please make a larger option!!!!  Mine never leaves my bed. I just push it to the end of the bed & go to sleep & wake up the next morning & pull it back towards me & resume my crafting or bills or whatever I was doing the night before. Lol.  Oh- and sometime- yeah SOMETIMES- I even use it as a food tray. Imagine that!! Lol.
product: Texas Instruments TI-84 Plus Graphing Calculator

text: I expected to love this journal Bc I love the color & I absolutely love glitter. However, what arrived is a hot mess. It’s hard as a rock on one entire side. There is no fluid & there is absolutely no consistency in this product. All of the glitter is bunched up & melted/solidified to one side of the journal. It’s vile. This was supposed to be a gift. Thank heaven I had it delivered to me for wrapping Bc I would have been absolutely mortified for the recipient to have received this & equated it’s total lack of quality with my feelings for the recipient.  I do not even understand how a product in this condition is being shipped by the manufacturer or Amazon. Shame on both of them. It’s been a complete waste of my time & my $ (spent looking for the item, waiting for the item, paying for the item & now having to return the item)!  Unreal this is offered for sale in this condition. Not cool ppl.
product: Texas Instruments TI-84 Plus Graphing Calculator

text: Aside from the fact that this was obviously dropped while being packed bc it has dirt on it & is so damaged I cannot believe it was even shipped I still cannot believe how lacking in quality these are.  I ordered two & while the second is in a little better shape I already know this will be a return for me. As a disabled person I tend to take falls & maybe the bad packaging person that dropped the book did me a favor by pointing out its obvious flaws.  The main reason I purchased these books to begin with was because the company donates a book for every book you buy and I always try to support companies that give back in this manner & to this degree, but for my personal needs these just won’t work. They just are not sturdy enough.  If they had arrived I damaged & I could have used them for a while & found that out for myself I probably would have given them a higher rating, but I think 3 stars is fair as they are usable for most oriole & priced okay currently. They are actually cheaper today than when I purchased them days ago.  Amazon always seems to lower prices after I purchase stuff. Lol. So much for Irish luck. Lol.  These will do for grocery lists or general to do lists, but you won’t want to trust your poetry or fine writing to these imo. The paper is very thin & see through & some of my pages are glued together weirdly. Idk why & it’s driving me crazy. Definitely a return.
product: Texas Instruments TI-84 Plus Graphing Calculator

text: Not the best pics, but I bought these to give to my kids & grandkids along with these pens I’m making for them with resin. I used temporary tattoos & then I sprayed a clear urethane over them so they the gold tattoos don’t run off & I make sure to place the tattoos above & below the rubber band thingy.  For less than $4 a kid my kids have a great planner that’s refillable & I can personalize each with animals they  like & I have since added their names using the creep $1 alphabet stickers you can buy most places.  I also do this with the black sketch books as you can see in one of the pics bc they can use them to sketch or for kites or to journal depending on the sketch book you buy. These are $3 black Daler Rowney drawing sketch books with Amazon bought temporary tattoos.  I sent my kids pics of the tattoos & let them pic their designs. The alphabet beads were also purchased on Amazon & I threaded them into the ink refills bc they kept trying to float up from the resin. I actually had to glue them to the refill bc the air spaces still made them want to float, but it was so much fun. It was my first foray into resin which didn’t go great die to some bad resin at first, but finally got some good stuff & had some fun.  I know I got off topic, but you can’t go wrong with these little journals imo.

text:
I was really excited to receive my new clipboard to replace my previous one which had started falling apart! My first thought on opening the box was "wow, this is huge". It's longer than my previous clipboard, so I wasn't sure how I would like toting it around at work (I'm an RN). However, the difference has been like trading up from a rickshaw to a Caddy. Not that there is anything wrong with a rickshaw!

My new clipboard fits everything but the kitchen sink (see attached pic). I wear my stethoscope, but can store it in the clipboard when I leave work, so I don't forget it at home and then have to wear one of those plastic yellow isolation-cart throwaway ones that don't work for poop. My pockets are usually BUSTING at the seams with all the stuff I like to carry around. No longer! I can cram it all inside this beast! I don't know how well it will hold up to my dropping it repeatedly, so I will post an update in a while with the durability factor.

P.S. I am one of those endlessly-researching people who look up reviews online for 7,456 items before deciding on one. You're welcome. I've done all the legwork for you. :-)

**Update, December 2016: This case is still perfect! No cracks, the top clip is still tightly secured, still feels really sturdy. :)

**Update, after dropping the case a few times over the years, a piece of the plastic broke. I'm buying a new one now. It lasted a really long time given the price!

text:

A fine point is actually the 'secret' of many artists today....the finer you can get the point, the more precise every square inch of your work will be. I personally prefer to use a standard card or one of the standard cards they sell which have holders in them. This package, containing two cards and envelopes, is expensive for what you get (and you aren't getting much).

text: First, let me state I am a Vine reviewer and received this pop-up tape as part of the program. I have used it for almost a month now exclusively for when I am wrapping things. Tape can never stick to itself based on the design (and I have in the past thrown away a lot of tape when it did). Pieces are a great size for wrapping presents Negatives:Only 74 pieces in the pack (so it's an expensive alternative to a regular roll of tape). If you are wrapping something where you want larger pieces of tape to hold things in place you are out of luck (these pieces are premeasured). text:
Overall, it’s a great quality. After receiving it, it sort of makes sense why its only 6 months since every day has two pages and the size.

This agenda is laid out in sections.

First Section: Address Book
Second Section: 6 full month calendars
Third Section: 26 week recaps
Fourth Section: 6 months of individual day pages
Fifth Section: Notes

I expected and would have preferred for it to be month, 7 days, week recap, 7 days, week recap, etc. and then go on to the next month. This may have been in the description but it caught me by surprised.

Overall, I’m going to keep and use it the agenda. It was a great price, seems sturdy, and it’s professional enough for me to use at work. I’m overall happy with my purchase, but the oddity of the layout and it only being 6 months when I was anticipating (and originally searched for) was a year long agenda is the reason for 3 stars. Who knows? I may end up liking this format better and buy another one in 6 months. :)

It holds him well, for at least another couple of months (after that I am sure he will be climbing out of it so I will need an alternate means). Highly recommend for all babies and children!
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: This is a great safety gate. I have a new puppy that I like to keep in my kitchen area when I am trying to get stuff down around the house and this is perfect. Easy to set up, fits my doorway perfectly and is strong enough when he pushes against it and jumping it stays totally in place. Super attractive, too. Highly recommend if you are looking for a gate for puppies or babies!
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: Not sure I really should rate these as 3 or 4 stars. First, my two year old grandson really liked these (but then again, he loves all sweets). Second, I do like the size of these, they are easy for young children to pick up and eat. I don't, however, like the higher caloric and sugar contents (not something I would typically keep in the house for him when he visits). These might be good for a special treat, but not something I would normally give him.
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: This is an absolutely adorable crib set. Great for both boys and girls, so it would make a wonderful shower gift for someone who does not yet know the sex of their baby to be. This goes great with my grandson's room decor filled with giraffes and animals. The quality of this set is terrific, too. Soft and washes easily and colors still look vibrant after washing this set at least a half a dozen times by now. Highly recommend this set for the little one in your life!
product: Pampers Swaddlers Newborn Diapers

text: I have two grandsons (ages 3 and 7) who come to visit me regularly in Florida. I wanted a great sunscreen that would be easy to apply and would last a long time (particularly around the water). This Coppertone Pure and Simple is just what I was looking for. It sprays on easily (great for squirming impatient kids) and it stays on even if they are in the pool. It is also SPF 50 which makes it truly protect their sensitive skin. Highly recommend!
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: This a great size pack of baby wipes/tissues. Easy to carry in your purse (or pocket) and nice to have with me on a day out with my 2 year old grandson. Great for simple meal or just standard cleanups or for diaper changes. Light fragrance and non-irritating to baby's delicate skin, I think every parent should not leave home without these. Highly recommend.
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: My new 3 month old grandbaby has super sensitive skin so I was anxious to try these. These wipes are a super nice size (which makes me have to use only one or two during a standard diaper change). They are moist and clean well, and do not have a scent. After using them exclusively for a week straight, I can report that baby's skin was soft and smooth and blotch free (something she has had problems with since birth). I just ordered another package and will be continuing to use them (this brand is far more economical than Pampers brand that I had been using). Highly recommend.
product: Pampers Swaddlers Newborn Diapers

text: This is perfect for when my 3 year old grandson comes to visit. I alternate it between my kitchen and bath and allows him easy access to sink and countertop. Washing hands becomes a breeze and helping Meemaw make cookies becomes one of his favorite pasttimes when he can stand next to me to help. Highly recommend this step stool!
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: This is a great stroller to use. I had been looking for a stroller to keep at my house for when my grandson (age 2) visits. This is lightweight, super easy to put together (comes almost 100% assembled) and my grandson fits perfectly in it. It has a great hood which helps block the Florida sun and it maneuvers perfectly. Highly recommend this stroller, it worked great in the stores and also in a park setting (dirt paths).
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: This is a high quality safety gate. It was easy to set up and use and it works great. I have a cat and a dog and i love the little door which allows my cat to get through (without trying to jump over it). My 80 lb. Irish setter mix stays carefully contained in the kitchen area while my cat can come and go as she pleases. This gate is relatively heavy, too, and cannot be knocked down easily. I used to have a gate years ago when my children were small but it was nothing of the quality of this gate. I highly recommend.
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: Huggies have been my go-to diaper when my own children were small and now for my grandchildren. They are soft, non-irritating, very absorbent and do what their name says: hug your baby to minimize leakage. I particularly like this new box of a one month supply. I always have a supply in my home and it takes several week visits before I get even close to running out. The larger 200 count size saves me money as well. Definitely recommend Huggies to all moms and households.
product: Pampers Swaddlers Newborn Diapers

text: This is super cute. Well made, colorful and easy to hang, this is a great way to measure your child (or children's growth). I think this is one of the best gift items for a baby shower. Regardless of whether it is your first child or not, this chart can measure all your children. It is also easy to write on to measure the life/growth of all your children and would make a lifelong keepsake for any family. Highly recommend!
product: Pampers Swaddlers Newborn Diapers

text: Nice baby monitor for the money. My new grandbaby was visiting this past weekend and I set it up in my spare bedroom. When baby was napping, my son and daughter in law and I were able to sit out on the patio and still be able to monitor sound and movement in the room. Wish the sound was a little louder and the display was a little larger, but, this would make a great baby shower present or just a gift for a new parent.
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: These are just ok diapers. I usually use Pampers (or in a pinch Huggies) so I wasn't sure what to expect from this brand. I didn't find these as of the quality of the more expensive named brands. They are definitely less absorbent, the last time my grandson wore these it wasn't long before I found he was wet and his pajamas needed to be changed. Although they are soft and look good, they just aren't as great as the better brands. Maybe if you changed them more frequently they would be better, but then you might as well spend more and get Pampers. These are good in a pinch, but I wouldn't buy them on a regular basis.
product: Pampers Swaddlers Newborn Diapers

text: I love using Pampers on my two grandsons and have been using Pampers almost exclusively since they were born. This baby-dry variety is wonderful and in a size 5 fits my almost 3 year old grandson perfectly. They hug his legs with no leaks or irritations and they keep up with his active lifestyle. Highly recommend these for the baby you love!
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: First, I have to tell you that I used Pampers only exclusively for my children and now my grandchildren. I love the way the fit and keep babies dry. Second, they are easy to put on and remove and they are just perfect for your babies in all sizes and shapes. What I really love about these Pampers is the new size pack. With 150 count, they are more economical and will last me for quite a long while. I have already ordered another pack for my grandson out in California so my son's family can also benefit from the larger size box. Highly recommend for your babies and grandbabies!
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: Love this large economical size of Pampers Swaddlers. Same great quality but in a 70 pack which allows for less purchases throughout the month and costs you less in the long run. I almost exclusively use Pampers on my grandchildren, you can always depend on them for hugging their skin, preventing leaks and one of the highest absorption diapers in the market today. I am thrilled that Pampers finally started producing some of the largest sizes and also provides them in a larger package!
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: Pampers have been my go-to diapers for both of my grandchildren. I feel they fit my grandsons better and keep them drier than any other disposable diaper on the market. These Swaddlers are a great diaper for my oldest grandson. He is at a toilet training age, yet still has accidents. So, we use these diapers when we are out of the house on him to help in case he does have an accident. They fit him perfectly with no leg gaps and the diapers definitely prevent leakage. I highly recommend Pampers Swaddlers for the child in your life!
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: When it comes to teething aids, these are a good biscuit. My grandbaby did use it for a while and eventually bored of it. To be honest, I don't like that it is vegetable flavored and that it is made in China. Probably won't be purchasing them again (unless they produce a higher quality product). Rating it 4 stars as my grandson did chew on it for a while and it seemed to help with the urge to chew on his newly sprouting teeth. Just think I can do better elsewhere.
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: These are a really cute alternative to Pull-Ups. Work well, absorbs a lot and my granddaughter loves the pattern. Easy to pull up, my granddaughter is going through potty training with a breeze. She feels like such a big girl now that she is no longer in diapers. These are great and are less expensive than the named brands. Highly recommend!
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: It's hard to find a larger size diaper which works for my grandson. He is 2 1/2 years old and starting potty training. He loves to wear pull ups, but still has accidents. At night, I use these size 6 Little Movers for him and they work great. Super absorbent, soft, non-irritating and fit him great, these are a great transitional diaper to help him make the move to being fully independent. I have loved Huggies diapers for all my grandchildren and these are great for the older child who isn't quite yet ready to be on his own.
product: Pampers Swaddlers Newborn Diapers

text: These are just perfect for my very active 2nd grandson. They are extremely absorbent and hug his body perfectly so there are never any leaks. Huggies to me are as good, if not better, than Pampers and usually cost us less. These Little Snugglers are definitely our favorite variety and we love this larger pack as well (more economical). He will definitely be using these until he's old enough for the pull up variety. Kudos to Huggies for another great diaper!
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: My 2 1/2 year old grandson (and I) love these Huggies pull up potty training pants. First, the colors and Disney design are great and he loves wearing them. Second, they are made very well and look regular underwear. He loves to be praised when he successfully stays dry during the night but these training pants are great for the times when he 'forgets' . We have used Huggies on and off for diapers when he was an infant, but now that he is older and training, Huggies are the way to go. Super absorbent, don't leak, and for overnight use they are great. We highly recommend!
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: My 2 1/2 year old grandson (and I) love these Huggies pull up potty training pants. First, the colors and Buzz Light Year design are great and he loves wearing them. Second, they are made very well and look regular underwear. He loves to be praised when he successfully stays dry during the night but these training pants are great for the times when he 'forgets' . We have used Huggies on and off for diapers when he was an infant, but now that he is older and training, Huggies are the way to go. Super absorbent, don't leak, and for overnight use they are great. We highly recommend!
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: My 2 1/2 year old grandson (and I) love these Huggies potty training pants. First, the colors and Disney design are great and he loves wearing them. Second, they are made very well and look regular underwear. He loves to be praised when he successfully uses the bathroom, but these training pants are great for the times when he 'forgets' . We have used Huggies on and off for diapers when he was an infant, but now that he is older and training, Huggies are the way to go. Super absorbent, don't leak, they are great. We highly recommend!
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: This is a great back pack, good for multiple users. I had my daughter-in-law (who has a 4 year old and a 9 month old) use this so I could get her feedback. Bottom line, she absolutely loved this back. Not overly big, but packs a punch when it comes to roominess and versatility. Quality made and stylish, she can use this when bringing both children out with plenty of room for diapers and wipes for the infant and room for snacks and juices for the older child. She also said she is able to pack some clothing and toys for use when needed. She told me she can also see using this when the kids are older just for overnight use or just for using around town. I highly recommend!!
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: These are great diapers for my very active two and a half years old grandson. You can always trust the Pampers name for quality and fit. Sure, they may cost more than generic diapers, but in the case of disposable diapers you truly get what you pay for. These pull ons are great transitional diapers being used while he is toilet training (especially overnight) and the pull on feature prepares him for traditional underwear. Soft, very absorbent and conforms well to his legs, Pampers pullons are perfect for the toddler in your life. Kudos to Pampers for another great product!!
product: Pampers Swaddlers Newborn Diapers

text: These are really nice, lightly scented, full size baby wipes. One sheet should be able to cover an entire diaper change. My new grandbaby has such delicate skin and these are wonderful on his sensitive skin, cleaning him and leaving his bottom clean and smelling great. I love Huggies products (diapers and wipes) and use them almost exclusively when he is visiting. I highly recommend them for the child that you love!
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: Another amazing Amazon brand product. This is comparable to named brands (think J&J) but at a lesser cost. I always keep a bottle of this in my master and guest bathrooms for when my two grandsons visit. They have both been bathed with this wash since they were infants and they have never had a tear shed during the use. Their skin smells fresh and clean and their hair is soft and shiny after use. I highly recommend for anyone who has children (or likes to  use a mild shampoo/wash for sensitive adult skin).
product: Pampers Swaddlers Newborn Diapers

text: I have never tried Grab Green products before. I really like this Natural Baby Lotion in a chamomile fragrance. First, it smells great. Second, it goes on my grandson's skin smoothly and is absorbed quickly. I love after applying it to him rubbing my hands together and allowing my own skin to absorb it as well. His skin feels great after use and despite he tends to have sensitive skin, there are no adverse side effects from using this lotion. I highly recommend.
product: Pampers Swaddlers Newborn Diapers

text: Funny, I liked the Honest Company diapers I reviewed earlier this month, but these training pants are terrible. First, they are huge on my grandson. Second, there are noticeable gaps around the legs which really don't help to retain moisture at all. I think they feel cheap, too, in using them as a pull up. I am afraid if I pulled them too hard they would rip. I am not sure if it's just the one package I got or if they are all like this. I see a bunch of other negative reviews too, so I think you might want to steer away from these and spend a few extra dollars for a named brand product.
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: These diapers work really well. No leaks, grip my grandson in all the right places (with no irritation) and they are super absorbent. I love the fact that these are a cheaper alternative to the 'name' brands (Pampers/Huggies) without losing any of the quality or functionality. And my grandson absolutely loves the T-Rex print on the diapers (and actually looks forward to getting a diaper change, lol).
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: I am a grandmother with three grandchildren, 2 of whom are still in diapers. For all three, Pampers Swaddlers have been a staple in my household (when they visit) and my son's household for over 6 years now. Pampers knows babies. This Swaddlers variety is great. It hugs the baby and is very absorbent. They have and do fit my grandbabies perfectly and I never worry about leakage or rashes from poor fitting diapers. We are for sure a Pampers family, wouldn't use any other variety in our households.
product: Pampers Swaddlers Newborn Diapers

text: These training pants are an alternative to Pampers and Huggies. Unfortunately, they run a little higher in price and are not of the same quality. I used these on my 2.5 year old grandson when my son's family was visiting for the past 10 days. I had no trouble with fit (they seemed fine). I didn't think they were as absorbent as other diapers and I found myself reverting back to Pampers on the overnight. I am rating them at 4 stars, but could have just as easily done these as a 3 star, but they are cute with the animal print and my grandbaby loved them. Not sure I will by them again unless there is a discounted product I just can't resist.
product: Pampers Swaddlers Newborn Diapers

text: These diapers are great. I got them to have here in the house when my 2 year old grandson was visiting. My daughter-in-law has been a Pampers user for all of her children, so I was afraid when she saw these she would not want to use them. However, surprisingly she did try them and to my surprise actually liked them. These are just as absorbent and fit my grandbaby as well as Pampers, and with one exception are comparable to their cruisers cousins. The price! Given that she now has three children, all different ages and at different stages of their training, these Amazon Brand are a great way for her to still 'pamper' her baby while saving much needed cash. After using them exclusively for the weekend, she asked me for the link of where to get these (so she could use them when she got home). I ended up buying her a pack and shipping it to her so it was there when she got home. But I know she is sold on these now and will most likely change to the Amazon brand when she goes for her next order.
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: I have two grandchildren (ages 4 and 1 1./2) and I thought I would give them a go while they were visiting over the Christmas holidays. The older child didn't really care for them, the younger one did. I tried a piece myself and found it a little dry and not very flavorful (maybe it's the lack of sugar in it). Anyway, these are all organic and are made for healthy snacking. For that, I will give these 4 stars. I believe that they are made for the youngest of toddlers, whose tastebuds are still maturing and are more into the texture and the fact that they are able to feed themselves.I am going to try another one of their varieties for the next time as I like the way these are made and I am hoping I will make a hit with both grandchildren next time.
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: This was my first time using Mountain Falls products, so to be honest, I was slightly skeptical. My 7 month old grandbaby was here for the weekend and typically he has problems with rashes (sensitive skin). I ended up putting this side by side with his typical ointment and figured I would try the Mountain Falls first. I loved the light fragrance on this product and it goes on easily and totally protected his skin and private part areas. My daughter in law, when picking him up Sunday night, asked me what I had been using and I shared this with her. She asked if she could take it with her for the rest of the week and I happily obliged. Getting it back more than half empty, I readily ordered another to have it here for Labor Day weekend when they are returning again. I can highly recommend this for babies with sensitive skin for full protection and helps to let skin heal quickly and rashes to almost vanish within a few days.
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: My grandson loves these. He is three years old and is at the stage where he is almost toilet trained, but needs to wear these occasionally (like when we are out and about and we can't count on him holding it). These easy ups look like underwear and pull up like regular underwear. He likes feeling like a 'big boy' and actually loves wearing them. We occasionally use these overnight as well and they are super absorbent with no leakage through the night.he loves the colorful characters as well. We have been a Pampers family since my son was born and now we are using them for my grandson. Pampers have always been high quality and designed to please kids and parents! We would not use any other brand!
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: I was looking for a gate that would help contain my new puppy and allow them some room while training (without using a cage/crate). This gate is just wonderful. Perfect height and fits perfectly in my kitchen entrance. Securely fits in the doorway and even with my pup jumping and pushing on it holds him in securely. I love the mesh screening as it allows total visibility both ways Easy to open and close (even for someone like me with slightly arthritic hands. No complaints just compliments at what a great gate this is for the money.
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: I typically use a lot of Dove products in my home, mostly for my own use. But, now that I have my two grandsons visiting more frequently (3 and 1 years old), I had been looking for something for their sensitive skin. This Baby Dove product hits the mark easily. First, it has a very light fragrance and it is light and easy to apply (a little goes a long way). It perfectly cleans and moisturizes their skin and they feel wonderful after their baths. My one year old grandson is having some problems with diaper rash and I will say that I was initially wondering if this might burn or irritate him, but it didn't. I highly recommend this tip to toe wash for all mothers to use during baby's bath time. Just perfect!
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: I have been using these over the last two weeks while watching my 7 month old grandson. I honestly can say that I have no idea what the one star reviews are about. I hate to admit that typically I use Pampers products when he visits, but since I had the opportunity to use these Huggies I was thrilled to try something new. My grandbaby is a big boy who loves his bottle. No leaks on the overnight and he slept most nights straight through (even with a wet diaper). I highly recommend these to anyone who has been looking for an absorbent overnight diaper for the child (or grandchild). Kudos to Huggies for giving me a great alternative!
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: This is a great organizer for the mom on the go. It's great to have a place to keep drinks stored (instead of having to hold them) and it's insulated as well (which allows me as a grandmother to always keep my grandson's juice pack cold when he is down here with me in Florida). I also like the detachable bag which is great to carry some change/money and any other small items you might want (so you don't need to carry a handbag with you when you are walking. Fits great on the City Mini GT stroller I keep at my house for visits and looks great too. Great item to give as a shower or baby present for the mom who has everything.
product: Pampers Swaddlers Newborn Diapers

text: I absolutely love these pull-ups. First they are well made super absorbent and super easy to get on. My grandbaby loves them because they are colorful, have cartoon characters which he loves and the style (getting him out of the old diapers) makes him feel like a 'big boy', These are great for that transition stage between diapers and regular underwear, and my grandson loves to wear these (and show off the cartoons, lol). Can't get a better training pant out there anywhere!!!
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: I am a huge fan of Graco products, own several to have at the house for when my grandchildren come to visit. Quality, durability and last for many many years. You can rely on Graco as a safe, quality product for children. I love this Jetsetter, I set it up relatively easily (and trust me I am not technically capable for many items to put together). This took me about 15 minutes to assemble out in the yard. I love the fact that I can actually put the canopy on it to keep the sun of my grandson when he is playing in it. I do agree with others, I don't see this too much as a portable device unless you are relatively strong. I believe parents would capable of carrying this around (my daughter-in-law amazes me sometimes at the things she can cart). I, on the other hand, struggled with the 20+ lbs and had some difficulty trying to get it in to the carry on bag (my son had to help me. Outside of the weight, I love this playard. You can use it for naptime inside, bring it outside to keep baby safely occupied while you do chores. Would make a great baby shower present for the mom to be.
product: Pampers Swaddlers Newborn Diapers

text: I love Graco products, use them almost exclusively for my grandchildren when they visit. When I first received this, I immediately put it in my car (Fiat) for me to use for the following weekend when my 2 year old grandson was coming. I am rating this as only 4 stars as it was extremely difficult for me to get hooked into the car and I had to wait for my son to do it. I thought at first it might be me, or the car, but my son also had difficulty with the plastic clasping device. However, once he was able to hook it in, it worked great. My grandson fit in it comfortably and I love the fact that this will grow with him for years to come (and I won't need to purchase any others). As I mentioned, it was difficult to install (hence the rating) but overall, it's a great car seat, very attractive and versatile (for ages).
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: This was a perfect sippy cup for my 16 month old grandbaby. He loves to eat and drink and typically makes a mess with everything he touches, lol. I found this Munchkin Miracle cup perfect for him. No spills or leaks and he loves independently being able to drink at the table like a 'big boy'. This would make a great baby shower gift since often times we only think only of the newborn when it comes to gift planning (no one thinks of what will be coming).
product: Pampers Swaddlers Newborn Diapers

text: I specifically ordered this stroller at the advent of my grandson coming down to visit me in Florida for two weeks. And it couldn't have arrived at a better time. First, let me tell you I am a very technically challenged 50 something year old woman. This Graco Breaze is a breeze to put together! I love the maneuverability of it, especially considering getting around tight places. I took him to Disneyland and used this exclusively. The rotating wheels handled the ins and outs of crowds like a charm. This unit is lightweight (in other words, easy for me to carry) and folding it up with one hand makes it extra easy when you have a child in tow. I also love the features of the cup holders and the pockets for storage. The seats are cushioned and the canope top kept my little grandson out of the harmful Florida UV rays. I always have loved Graco products (in fact, I purchased one of the Play yards for him to sleep in when he visits. They are made to be durable, long lasting and hold up to a lot of abuse. This stroller will probably work well for him for the next couple of years being that it can hold up to a 50 pound toddler. And the cushioning and the adjustability of the seating is a great feature to grow with. I love this Graco Breaze!
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: I have reviewed several other monitors on Amazon. I can safely say that I do not think the Summer Wide View is as good as some of the others. Without belaboring what others have said, I find the feed to be choppy and uneven. Although the screen is a great size and the definition is great (when it works), I believe the minuses are making me only give this monitor 3 stars, I think you can best spend money on other monitors or even stay in the Summer family but upgrade to a much better monitor. I find this would be good for a definitive close in source (i.e. just the crib alone) but not if you are looking to monitor a large room (or expect to pan to other areas without quality loss).
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: I keep these at my house and use them for my grandson when he comes to visit on the weekends.They smell beautifully and clean thoroughly. I honestly can't vouch for the sensitive skin part of these (my grandson does not necessarily have 'sensitive' skin). But I will say that I have yet to encounter any diaper rash issues and he is one happy baby!
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: First, this bassinet is easy to set up and is so beautiful! The muted pastel colors among the bright white background make this bassinet fit in almost any room or decor. The mattress is perfect and the bonus motion and music/sounds make it absolutely perfect for a newborn. My grandbaby (who is a little over a month old) has a tendency to be a bit collicky and this vibrating bassinet is just perfect to rock little Talon to sleep. I highly recommend this for any new mom or mom to be. I think my grandson will easily get another 2-3 months of use with this beautiful bassinet and we couln't be happier. This Summer unit is a must have!
product: Pampers Swaddlers Newborn Diapers

text: Only pro I can give this device is that it looks great and the description and features look very tempting. When I first read the description, I thought this would be a great device to use with my son's iphone for use with his new baby when they were spending time here last month. Well, I couldn't be more disappointed. App keeps freezes (read the other reviews on here) and my son eventually stopped even trying to use it. Spend some extra money and go for a better device if you truly love your child (or grandchild). Not worth it, even as a vine freebie.
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: These are the greatest! I work on a TV show and the sound stages get stuffy and dusty. The whole crew- and actors too- pass the boogie wipes all around! We all love them! Get a 3 pack- in your car, by the bed, in purse/ at work! Love 'em!! Cool and soothing! You'll be hooked!
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: Very nice, but took a little beating during the washing process. Still BEAUTIFUL though.
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: I bought this after I got a Graco Playard for my little granddaughter.  I cut some 2" thick foam to soften the floor a little bit so she could sleep in it & the sheet fits perfectly or I should say the foam piece was just the right size.  It's comfortable for her & I can quickly take it out if she just wants to play in her playard.
product: Pampers Swaddlers Newborn Diapers

text: I bought this for my precious granddaughter. It's easy to clean, she can hold it & chew on it without any problems. It's very colorful & it rattles. This keeps her interested.
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: love it to clean my big 32oz drinking mason glasses and large coffee mugs !  i love that has a little suction cup holder that just sticks to my sink  very practical
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: We tested this by laying it on the couch. It did alert us of no breaths, it vibrated, and kept alerting us til we put it around our preemie. We bought this & the Nora sock. This will be a lifesaver if he stops breathing. Love it
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: My daughter loved the nipple cream and wanted to order a full size, but I accidentally threw away the empty sample pack. I wish I knew what the name of it was
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text:
Bought this to make latte's at home. It work great to froth up the milk. I also found the other mixing attachment handy for mixing up flour in water.

I would highly recommend this little gem.

I do not have high hopes on the battery cover hince the 4 stars vs 5. It's okay now but doesn't feel very durable.

text: These are small glass jars - with plastic lids and silicone gaskets.  Because the bodies are glass, they dishwasher safe - I have a small top rack of my dishwasher - so the lids with the gaskets in the lids went up there - but... I will say - you need to remove the silicone gaskets and wash them separately - meaning remove the gaskets from the lids.  Kinda a hassle - but better for thorough cleaning.  The lids twist down solidly - be careful not to miss set them before twisting.  The plastic lids will not last as long as the bodies because they are plastic - but for my purposes that's ok.  I use them to store little bits of liquid leftovers - etc.  They old about 2 oz jars. The lids are a lime like green - it's bright. They do NOT stack reasonably - without lids.. You can stack maybe 2 (total) with lids on - tipsy without - again, 2 only.  Comes with some disposable labels - about 12 or so.  But I don't use them. text:
I needed a night light with a timer for my 6 year old grandson... this seemed right. It is charged via a USB cord. Must be charged each night - eh. So I just leave it plugged in with a long usb cord. Makes you want to smack or wack it to get it to work. Eh. The colors are soft - there is a dimmer button. The timer is 15 mins, 30 mins or 60 mins options. text: I have a 15 year old VERY sweet dog who has been having issues lately. No matter how expensive the dog food I buy (organic, grain free, human grade, RX grade)  it doesn’t matter, she was still getting sick. I bought this and placed it on top of her food and she ADORES it! It’s easy for sick dogs to digest because it’s baby food. It’s more liquid than paste (like thick pea soup) and she laps it up. I couldn’t find any organic baby food in the store that had chicken in it for her in a squeeze pouch. This gives her low sodium, protein and veggies. There are 5 ingredients. Look at the back of Hills RX Science Diet and you will be horrified at what is in that food! This is much better for a dog with a sensitive tummy. I’m sure it’s great for babies but she is my baby. When dogs are sick they recommend baby food and this is the best.
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: I’m about three and a half months into using this thing and feel like it’s been long enough to form a solid opinion on it. If I had to do it again, I would definitely spend more time shopping around and would go with something else. So all of those other reviews that mention that it starts making an obnoxious noise? Yeah, happening here too now. It is now making this mechanical squeaking/clanking sound. Sometimes fiddling with the legs helps it to stop for awhile. I’ll give it three stars since it’s been extremely helpful to have until now, my baby loves the swing and unfortunately she is quickly growing out of it already (very tall) and for the price, I feel like we’ve gotten good use from it. The bouncer feature is pretty handy as well if you want a place to put baby down in the kitchen/bathroom etc... it’s easy to detach one handed. If I knew what I know though, I absolutely would have read more reviews and found one that would potentially fit her for longer, even if that meant spending more on the swing. To clarify, the size issue is not a mark against the swing, I just have a big baby who is growing like a weed. It seems like the sound started around when she hit about 15lbs. Not sure if the sound is related to weight or just the amount of time that it’s used though
product: Baby Brezza Formula Pro Advanced

text: Have a baby that spits out her Zantac and acts like you’re feeding her poison, this magic pacifier works great. She takes the medicine easily now. text: As several reviewers have mentioned, this is very well padded and soft, so should be comfortable for your infant.  The color is very pleasing, so it is an attractive car seat.  You can find seats that are half the price of this one, but you get what you pay for.  I am as cheap as they come, but this is one item that you probably don't want to skimp on, as it is important for the safety of your baby.  This claims to be rollover tested.  Not sure what that means unless it means that if your car has a rollover accident, the baby won't fall out of the car seat.  That could avoid serious injury or death.  This seat seems to be very strong and substantial.  It is seems a little heavy, but that's to be expected with the strong construction.  I don't know that much about baby car seats, but this one appears to be a very good one.  One reviewer mentioned that the inside is removable for washing.  That in itself seems like it is worth a little extra price.  In time, the insides of baby car seats can get pretty dirty, especially as the baby drools or spits up.  Like so many things these days, this is made in China, which may or may not be a concern to you.
product: Pampers Swaddlers Newborn Diapers

text: I am reviewing this from a little different perspective than most people.  This is advertisted as a gift set for parents with newborns.  I used it that way and the receipient was very greatful and was sure they could put the bottles to good use for their newborn.  I think it is one of the most practical gift sets you could give new parents.  Not only that, but it seems like a good value for what is included.  It is under $25 and it is a 12 piece set with 2 each of 2 different sized bottles, two cleaning brushes (bottle and valve), a pacifer, 2 pump adapters (fitting most common breast pumps) and 4 sealing discs (for storing and saving expressed breast milk).  The bottles include anti-colic valves, which is something I hadn't seen before; maybe it is common now, but bottles I am familiar with never had that.  Comes in an attractive box, so doesn't really need to be gift-wrapped.  BPA free.  Made in China, which is a slight concern, but it is a USA company.
product: Pampers Swaddlers Newborn Diapers

text:
This carseat is easy to install. The button to put the handle down is conveniently located at the top, middle of the handle vs having to push buttons on both sides of the carseat to move it. The special quiet feature is wonderful, too. There is no clicking sound when moving the cover or the handle. No waking up baby with clicking! The cover moves to the feet of the baby to cover the whole carseat, leaving a screen area so you can still see the baby.

The seat meets the new standard of a hard head support instead of just a cushion. This is a great safety feature.

Like all infant carseats, this is not light in weight, but it's a very safe seat and I recommend it.

Also, it's almost like a lifejacket instead of a hard plastic feeling under the baby.

text:
This stroller is nice. It rolls smoothly and can make sharp turns. The boys seemed comfortable and wanted to keep riding, they are 2. It's not as wide as some side by side strollers.

To recline the seat is just a buckle strap that you have to loosen to recline partially or unbuckle to recline fully. Kind of a pain to have to loosen for partial recline.

Each seat has it's own pouch attached at the top back along with a netted pouch lower for a total of 4 pouches to put items in. There are no baskets underneath. The cup holder for the parent is a nice plus.

text:
We have a newborn, and she recently has her first cold.

We have a bulb aspirator, but the bulb fails in comparison to this nasal aspirator. The gourd shaped nozzle is not terribly intrusive, and the cone shaped nozzle is great for getting deep into her sinuses.

Our baby surprisingly really enjoys having her boogery nose sucked out.

When using the aspirator I would suggest putting it on the lowest suction, and then turning it higher when actually in the nose so that it doesn’t get suctioned to the outside of the baby’s nose when using it.

It has honestly brought our baby a lot of relief and definitely less choking while feeding.
product: Pampers Swaddlers Newborn Diapers

text: My kids are grown, but I still find that I use baby wipes. I like to keep a package in the car to use. I work in a special needs classroom. Then if the clean up requires something stronger, than one of the adults will take care of that. These baby wipes are as expected. The case comes with 6 packages, each has a resealable lid. text: For a mid range stroller and car seat this is a great stroller and car seat. The cushioning on the stroller and car seat is not as thick as the Chicco one, but seems thick enough. I really like all the features of this stroller and wish I had it with my first two. I really like how it converts to a carriage/bassinet. Perfect for newborns. It also converts and can be switched around very easily. It was pretty straightforward to put together. No tools required, just a few parts to snap together and buttons to close. It is very lightweight and sleek looking and I'm hoping to get a lot of years and use out of it. I have also received complainants on it. My daughter likes riding in it and it also sits higher than her last stroller which she seems to like.
