text:
Update, it seems the seller did not care for my review, and contacted me!
Didn’t seem to like about this not being all cotton on my review.
This is my Honest Review.
—————
Ok child’s pillow with cute pillow case.
Good size

I ordered this child’s pillow for myself, as I was injured in a car strange accident and as time has Gone on for a year now my neck now is big problem.

I washed the pillow case as suggested and put it in the dyer. It came out ok.Also wash the pillow, both go in the washer & dryer as it says.
Before washing I noticed some strings attached , but made well.

The pillow cover is softer then the pillow case, which surprised me.
The pillow was delivered in a nice box, and the pillow is in a plastic bag with the air sucked Out of it. Once opened it takes a few mins for the pillow to expand. I shook it.
The size is good, can also be used for car trips for a child, or adult, and does not take up allot of space.

I guess since it said it was Cotton I was expecting a softer pillow, and the pillow stuffing
To be cotton, but it’s Not, the stuffing is a Polyester fiber!
It’s not cushy, and cuddly as I expected.
The only part of this that is cotton is the pillow case , although doesn’t say it is!
One of my grandchildren has the Kapok child filling pillow, that is very cuddly but this does not come close!
Although Kapok is about double the price or more.
I think any child would like this size pillow as there own.

I’m not changing my 3 star.
Made in China.
product: Pampers Swaddlers Newborn Diapers

text:
I ordered PEDIALYTE ADVANCEDCARE PLUS ELECTROLYTE POWDER (with 33% More Electrolytes and PreActiv Prebiotics) with the intention of using it as an electrolyte-replacement drink that is “ healthier” than sugary sports drinks (e.g., Gatorade). Unfortunately, I really dislike the taste of “Berry Frost” Pedialyte powder (dissolved in water). It has a strong, very artificial “fruit” flavor that fails to hide the medicinal purpose of the powder.

Each packet of Pedialyte “Berry Frost” powder has about 3-1/2 teaspoons of powder, to be dissolved in 16 oz. of water. One packet has 50 calories (12g total sugars).

Pedialyte Advanced Care is intended to rapidly replenish fluids and electrolytes that may be lost from dehydration due to vomiting and diarrhea, heat exhaustion, intense exercise, or travel. I will continue to use the product, but I’ll also be holding my breath when I drink it, so as to taste it as little as possible.
product: Pampers Swaddlers Newborn Diapers

text:
This MANHATTAN TOY FARMER’S MARKET SOFT ACTIVITY BOOK is kind of strange. It has pages made of silky fabric, with crinkle paper inside that only makes the pages feel odd (thick and clumsy). A few pages have different textures (e.g., a felt pea pod, nappy chickens), but the texture is almost entirely silk.

The cartoonish drawings of fruits and vegetables are cute, but the apples look like radishes, and the mushrooms are almost unrecognizable. The mushroom rattle barely makes a sound. There aren't very many pages, and the “story” isn't well thought out. The choice of things to count is random (stall, apples, chickens, etc.).

The lift-up variety features are illogical (an umbrella, a pea pod, a pocket for a mushroom rattle, an awning for a booth). It took me a while to figure out that the striped “1,2,3” flap on the front cover was an awning.

I question the educational value of an activity book with such hard-to-recognize elements (the awning, the vegetable drawings). This is supposed to be a travel toy, but I think an adult needs to explain it to a small child. I can't think of any reason for choosing this activity book out of the many that are available on the market.
product: Pampers Swaddlers Newborn Diapers

text: This has been a great playmat to have at my home for the grandkids. The youngest is just three months, and this has been perfect for his tummy time. The mat is very soft and colorful, with lots to attract little eyes. I also appreciate that the bottom of the mat is a smooth vinyl, protecting the floor from any mess, but also acting as a nice barrier between the floor and the baby. At three months, he's not using the attached toys yet, but my 2yo granddaughter *does* use them to engage with him and play the different songs and noises for him (which is really cute). I don't quite understand the need for the detachable part (or why I would hang this as a growth chart) - I will likely just keep this mat as one piece, since the bigger size is more useful.

text: Got this through the Vine program. Positives: Extremely attractive. I live in a townhouse with a relative small backyard. I don't have trees in my yard, so I use a decorative pole which I attached the Birdscapes feeder to. It looks absolutely gorgeous out there and the birds are out there daily. Having water and seed together is a new and unique concept to me as I have always just used a seeder (and let the birds get their water elsewhere, lol). I love watching the birds my window flock back and forth to both. It was easy to set up and has provided hours of enjoyment for my family, and my cat. I definitely see more birds in my yard having the combination of water and seed than I did with just my regular birdfeeders. Here are the negatives: There is nothing in this set up that will deter small wildlife (i.e. squirrels) from hanging and making a huge mess (with both the seed and the water. I try to offset this by keeping a small bowl on the ground filled with peanuts and other items that are attractive for the squirrels. I am thinking if this was hanging from a tree, and higher up off the ground, maybe it wouldn't be quite as accessible. However, there are other (and I have used them often) feeders that discourage the critters and make it difficult for them to access the seed. This is definitely not one of them. Overall, I recommend this set up simply because of its beauty and for the way I am seeing far more birds than ever in my yard.

text: This brush is awesome and effective.  The "brush" portion is soft, as you would expect.  It spreads nicely and then you toss in the top shelf of your dishwasher. As well as heat tolerance, I believe one of the best things about silicone is the ease of cleaning.  The handle is long, but not super long... eyeballing it.... about 12" inches or so?  The "brushes" are about 2" ?  I didn't find the length of the bristles or the handle to be too short or too long in use, both were just right to me. text:
I purchased this owl to try and deter ground squirrels from my herb box. I have pictures of them sitting right next to it. My little birds are not affected at all and that is good. I REALLY wanted this to work...
So, you unpack it. It's a good size, but it's very light weight. I placed it and stacked river rocks at the base so it wouldn't blow away in a good gust. As soon as I set in out the sun it the solar panel and the head began spinning like something out of the excorcist. I am pretty sure I've never seen an owl "naturally" move it's head like that... but what the heck, it moves. Also to note.... it makes a clunk sound right before it spins. And it spins at a fairly high speed. It does sort of bobble in the wind... a little. I had thought the movement would be effective... in theory... but my ground squirrels are just too smart. :)
Honestly, for $30 I should send it back as it clearly isn't a pest guard in any stretch of the imagination. But it is an funny yard trash element. Which is about the only reason to order it... or keep it.
I'm trying the "natural" smelly spray next. We'll see if that's more effective.
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text:
It should be easy to put together, but it took me 25 minutes because the holes were drilled very unevenly. Two of the screws attaching the rim to the base wouldn’t go in at all, but it still holds together well enough.

It’s a lot lighter than it looks, which is nice because it’s easy to move, but it doesn’t look cheap. The included fire poker has a very cheap-feeling handle. The inside rim of the fire bowl was scratched, but that won’t matter once it’s lit.

The grate almost lays directly on the fire bowl, so I don’t really get the point of it. There’s about a centimeter of space between the two. Kind of useless.

The spark guard lays perfectly and seems effective and safe, though I have yet to light it.
Overall, little bit of faulty manufacturing that you can work around, bit of a pain to assemble, but it’s really pretty and it seems safe.

text: My daughter saw a similar product online and wanted it. She loves the idea of seeing the birds close up. Set up was pretty easy. I downloaded the app on my phone. I was immediately able to see the feeder. At first we attached it to one of our trees. The squirrels found it to be a great location! So we moved it to the deck. Still, we only got squirrels. Then my husband got a metal pole from the garage. He attached a piece of wood to the top. Then he attached this to the pole. He put it in the middle of the yard, away from any trees. The darn squirrels shimmied up the pole without any difficulties. We need to grease the pole or something. We also need to replace the food as the squirrels have eaten it all. The system does work as it should. I haven't been able to use the identify feature yet as we haven't captured any birds on it yet. The camera unit has a cable so you can charge it. The one that my daughter saw which inspired her to have one of these, used solar power to charge it. That would probably be better as you wouldn't have to take out the camera to charge it. We're not giving up on this, we just need to work at keeping the squirrels away. Though we do find it amusing when the squirrels are on it!

text: My husband and I started keeping a bunch of bird feeders out in our backyard about a year ago. We were having to fill some of the feeders more than once a week. The squirrels were a lot of the problem, but the birds would also cause some of the spillage. With the stacks/cakes, we've seen almost no bird seed on the ground below. It also seems to last longer. While the stacks/cakes cost a little more, we're saving money because we're not having to refill the feeders as much. The stacks seem to last at least a couple of weeks. This larger one had a little breakage upon opening. I'm estimating about 5% of it was loose in the package. We saved that and put that out for the squirrels. We did have to buy a special wire hanger for this. It has a nice mixture of seeds and nuts. It's been popular with the birds!

text: We have a few humming birds that return to our yard every year. They're usually here from about March through October. I'm really excited to use these next year when they return. The first year we made our own sugar water concoction, but that took a lot of work. The next year we had a bottle that we had to dilute with water. Then this past year we had a big bottle of pre-made nectar that was ready to go, but we had to refrigerate it the unused liquid so it took up valuable space in the refrigerator. This product is really the easiest out there! It's basically a juice box for the birds! (or birbs as I recently learned from my teenage daughter.) Even though it's not humming bird season, I did go ahead and try one out. I watched the seller's short video, but it really wasn't necessary because it's so simple!! It's not messy at all. The instructions suggest replacing once a week, which isn't terrible. The box is decorated in bright flowers to attract the birds.  I like that the packaging is recyclable. Because the hummingbirds have left for the season, I can't attest to whether or not the hummingbirds like this, but I like it!
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text: This bird feeder is huge! It's much larger than I expected. It is pretty much a two person job to fill and hang this up. The handle part was difficult to put into the holes of the bar that goes through the tube. It was really pretty comical. We wish it would have come with an "S" hook or something similar. We ended up using one . It was near impossible to get it hung up without it. One of the product pictures showed it hanging with an "S" hook. We finally did get it hanging up, but not before spilling half of the birdseed onto the yard. I think it would be better if the top pieces were attached more permanently. text:
I mixed this in with some grass seed, without planting a tester batch, and threw this seed out on some dirt that was newly cleared from foxtail and stickers in my backyard.. I wish I hadn't... I am sad to report that probably closer to 99% (package says total of 1%) of what is coming in is weeds. Unfortunately one of the four or five different weeds/herbs is stinging nettle and I am once again fighting a weed that may cause injury/irritation to my dogs. I am trying to save the clover that is coming in, by hand weeding out nettle, but it is gonna be a battle that might be better won by clearing the whole spot and starting over again.

Update 10/26/19: In all my careful observation, I have only seen 3 actual clovers. Very disappointed. Water coming off. Letting it all die so we can clear and start again. Keeping the weed seed for the deep yard, it is ground cover, just not clover.

Planting a tester pot of these seeds to show proof it is the seed from the bag and not others from my yard. Will report back again when the tester pot germanates.

This is the future of Real Sustainability - no food is ever wasted! From the produce aisle, to fruits, and the meat and seafood department, we've created a solution that comes to the rescue - collecting it all just as it is expiring and would have otherwise been thrown away. Food waste from San Francisco, Los Angeles, Chicago, New York, Tampa, Houston, Atlanta, Seattle and major cities are now being collected and converted into OMRI listed organic fertilizers proudly offered by Dr. Earth. This means that what was once a landfill waste material can grow vegetables and trees and everything that grows in the soil all across America. Nothing is wasted and would-be waste is upcycled. Be a good partner with the Earth, and please Recycle, and use Organic when you can.

text:
I had two big truck loads of wood-chips delivered for the garden to keep the weeds down.

BIG Mistake. The wood chips where full of Carpenter Ants, and earwigs.
We dug into the pile of wood chips, and sprinkled Terro Ant Bait.
It does take a couple of weeks for the Ants etc. to take the bait back to there holes , and KILL the QUEEN..Yay..
Once the Queen is dead, the whole colony is dead...This does work.

I also sprinkle some under the flower pots, and places where the dogs or cat could not get to it.

It does take at least a couple of weeks to get the Carpenter ants, earwigs etc, to come out for this bait, take it back to
the Queen, and that is the end of your bug problem..
That said , new bugs will come from other new colonies, and you have to start with the bait again,,But this will kill the bugs you have now.
Just give it time, Terro does work...

I will always keep this in my garage, with other Terro products, cause they all work..
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

You can use this around your vegetables with out any fear.
I grow Organic Vegetables, and have no fear of using this product.

That said, it does work if you use at the right time of the year.. Early Spring, about 3 to 5 weeks before seeds start to sprout..It will not kill your daffodils etc that are started to come up.
Reapply in late summer, when it cools down..
You can also apply this at anytime, but that is the time of the year for more effects over seeds sprouts to die.

There is 22 or more types of weed seeds this will kill off, for next season. You can use it on your lawn with out fear
of killing it unless your lawn is Weeds, that you just mow over!

This 5 lb. bag will treat 250 sq ft, or a 25 x 10 area...It can get expensive, so pick your area wisely.
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text:
I think this looks like the picture, and it is nice looking.

The cushions look a bit different in different lights, although a little darker! But basically looks the same as the picture..

The bottom cushions are 6 inch thick, and the back cushions are 4 inch thick.With velcro to keep them in place.
Having thick cushions sounds like they would be plush to sit on, but there not.
They are hard. I have a bad back, so there fine for me, but if your thinking of taking a Snooze with some pillows on this love seat, you would not be comfortable.
For sitting outside there good . The love-seat has nice modern lines, which I like.

I have a outdoor sofa with the same fabric as the cushions. I take my vacuum outdoors, and they vacuum off clean, and look good.I also used a sponge after if there are any spots.
This does clean up nice. I Have a small dog, and cat who like to lay out on the outdoor furniture.
In the winter I'll bring this in the garage, and put the cushions in a big black plastic bag to store them..

My Husband put this together by himself ( a real miracle). He is NOT Handy at all, and he was yelling that the Alen Wrench that comes with this, did not match
the bolts that came with this at all. The wrench was useless. (he had a good reason to complain)
He did have the right alen wrench in the garage (what luck). Then some of the holes did not match up like they should!
The bolts should have also been longer. But finally this came together, and as I said , looks good.
I hope this holds up , or will'll have to buy longer bolds to match.

The back of the love seat has a nice curve to it, which gives this an expensive look.

This is a good value, and does feel sturdy. (hopefully). The rod iron is sturdy.
I think the love-seat holds 500 lbs.
If I remember right , this is made in China.

If my Husband can put this together by himself, then anyone can..It took about 30mins..(My Hubby is good guy, just Not a handy one)

I gave this 3 stars for the wrench not matching up at all, and the cushions being hard.. I do love the look of this loveseat, and it will add to our backyard seating, and look very nice.
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text: Any time we have ever had a mouse in the house, my husband would grab any and all the traps he could find but they were never effective until sticky trap. The sticky trap has worked every time. These are small but we caught 2 on the same trap. One mouse got too smart for it once so I placed a tiny piece of chocolate in the middle of the trap and it was stuck to it the very next morning.
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text:
This INSECTO FOOD GRADE DIATOMACEOUS EARTH (1/2 lb Resealable Bag) is a good choice if you want to control insect pests without using insecticides that contain toxic or environmentally-polluting ingredients. Diatomaceous earth is silica powder that sticks to the bodies of insects (e.g., ants, fleas), and kills them by dehydrating their outer shells.

Food grade diatomaceous earth is the purest, safest form to use. (The other forms are labeled as pesticide or for swimming pool use.) It’s okay to use food grade diatomaceous earth in areas where food is prepared, and okay to ingest in small amounts. Farmers and truckers use it to protect stored grains, so you’re probably already eating it in small quantities.

You want to avoid breathing in the dust from diatomaceous earth, because silica can irritate or harm your lungs. Use a mask when you apply the dust, and don’t leave piles of the stuff lying around.

Diatomaceous earth is always a bit messy to use. Try to work it into cracks and crevices, then sweep or vacuum up the residue. For more safety information, try googling for "diatomaceous earth".

I’ve found food grade diatomaceous earth to be effective at controlling ants, but it takes several days to work. Also, if you have a major ant infestation, you will have to resort to some form of toxic pesticide.
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text:
This ORTHO HOME DEFENSE FLYING INSECT KILLER AEROSOL (16 oz.) will kill the flies, gnats, and other insects listed on the label. However, you need to spray it directly on the insects, which isn’t easy when they’re on the wing. Used outdoors, the cloud of aerosol dissipates too quickly to kill larger insects when they are flying around--you must wait and hit them when they land somewhere.

A spray that drops flying insects in their tracks would probably be too toxic to be safe for humans. (Especially because aerosols usually blow back onto the person using them, even when the air seems still.) I keep this product on hand, because there are times when I need to target a particularly annoying bug.

A product that is surprisingly effective in my outdoor garbage-can area is this [[ASIN:B00IMHL9JO Black Flag HG-11015 Stick]]. Basically it is old-fashioned flypaper--extremely sticky, and it works.
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text:
I really love this MARTHA STEWART JAPANESE STAINLESS HORI (WEEDING) KNIFE (w/7.5-Inch Blade, Steel Hilt; Bay Leaf Green Wooden Handle). The blade has the same curve and pointed tip as my (formerly) favorite gardening trowel, but the blade is wider and longer, and it’s marked with inches for digging holes that stop at the correct depth for plants you’re setting out.

For weeding in Spring when the soil is soft, just insert the blade tip under a young weed, push down with minimum pressure, and the weed will flip out roots and all. You get wonderful leverage against weeds, with minimum effort.

The serrated and straight edges aren’t very sharp, so the knife isn’t very good for pruning anything but soft plants. However, the tip is extremely sharp, and overall the knife is a fabulous planting tool. It comes with a durable storage sheath.
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text:
This MOONRAYS HUNGRY ROTTEN JACK-O-LANTERN (97027 Solar, Weathered Orange) is a cute Halloween accent for your yard. During the day, the solar panels collect energy from sunlight; when it gets dark, the eyes glow a creepy yellow. The jack-o-lantern’s big teeth are funny during the day. It’s fairly small--a little under 6” high and about 5.5” wide.

The jack-o-lantern is made from polyresin, so it’s probably okay to leave it outdoors when it rains. The device comes with 1 rechargeable AAA battery already installed. It uses only Ni-MH rechargeable batteries.

The solar panels are in the back of the jack-o-lantern (behind the stem), so you need to place it in a spot where it will get overhead sunlight. The instructions recommend that it get 8 hours of sunlight for the longest period of nighttime illumination. It also has an on/off switch on the bottom that you can use to conserve the stored solar charge.
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text:
My two favorite local nurseries (Berkeley Horticultural, Annie’s Annuals) promote the full Dr. Earth line of fertilizers, so I know they’re good.

This DR. EARTH FLOWER GIRL BUD & BLOOM BOOSTER (1 lb) differs from ordinary flower-booster fertilizers, because it contains non-plant-food ingredients (e.g., bacillus licheniformis, glomus mosseae, laccaria bicolor, etc.) that are “designed to bring life to all soil types in backyard gardens or in container plants”. These “soil-feeding” ingredients are in addition to the usual balanced nitrogen-phosphate-potash (3-9-4) plant food that encourages flowering instead of leaf production.

The Dr. Earth Bud & Bloom Booster is easy to apply. You simply sprinkle several tablespoons onto a container plant, or a cup or so around an in-ground plant; work it lightly into the top layer of soil; and water thoroughly. No pre-mixing into a gallon or two of water is necessary!
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text:
Rodent repellent products like this VICTOR M806 MOUSE-AWAY (Mouse Repellent 1.75 lb) rely on “fragrances” that are unpleasant to targeted critters for effectiveness. Mouse-Away employs a combination of cinnamon, citric acid, and essential oils (castor, cinnamon, peppermint, citronella) to deter mice from entering your home around the foundation. It does NOT kill mice; it repels them.

You need to spread the pellets all around your home’s perimeter, and after that Mouse-Away will only work for a limited time—for as long as the odor remains fairly strong. The product needs to be reapplied regularly. Some mice won’t be bothered at all. I’ve had little luck with rodent repellers; traps still work best for me, and Victor makes great traps.

The reason to use Mouse-Away is to protect birds and other critters that feed on mice—if they consume mice weakened or killed by rodent poisons (e.g., warfarin) they can be poisoned, too. Mouse-Away contains no poisons, so it’s a reasonable choice for outdoor use, if you need to feel that you’re doing something to combat your outdoor mouse population.
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text:
These PATCH COMPACT SELF WATERING HERB & GREENS PLANTERS (3 Pack) are paper-like Tyvek planters that are fairly easy to assemble. They’re ideal for planting an herb garden in a small patio or on a balcony, or for starting seeds or seedlings to transplant to a big backyard garden. I think they’re kind of bulky (6” x 12”) for an indoor windowsill garden.

Note that you must buy your own potting mix to fill these planters. Potting mix is readily available, in bags of varying types and sizes, from garden stores or Amazon (see, e.g. [[ASIN:B00G8FEUJI Miracle Grow Moisture Control Potting Mix]]. You want potting mix, not garden soil, because potting mix contains things that retain water (e.g., peat moss). Potting mix is cheap enough, even in small bags, that you can afford to throw it away when you fold these planters for storage (though your garbage company may frown on your adding dirt to your trash).

Self-watering planters like these really work. You must pay some attention to them, though. If you don’t keep water in the water reservoir, your plants will dry out, same as if you didn’t water them. Self-watering planters are really good for seeds and seedlings, which love being kept uniformly moist.

The planters are cheap enough (about $20 for one, $50 for three on Amazon) that you can throw them away after each new growing season or crop, and dispense with the bleach-solution routine that’s usually recommended for replanting hydroponic gardens or plastic self-watering planters.
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text:
Add my voice to the chorus—this MAXFORCE QUANTUM 0.03% IMIDACLOPRID ANT CONTROL LIQUID BAIT (with 10 bait stations) really works when [[ASIN:B00JG77GUK TERRO Indoor Ant Bait]] loses its effectiveness.

My home is built above an ant colony that stretches for miles along the California coastline, so all I can hope for is to control the ants that come indoors. The ants come in with all major weather changes, such as the start of the rainy season, or sudden shifts from hot to cold weather. Originally I used Grant’s ant-killer products. When they lost effectiveness, I switched to TERRO products (indoors and outdoors), and they worked very well for several years.

However, California’s weather has been erratic for the past year or so, and the ants have been coming indoors so often, they finally established a beachhead inside the house walls. They’ve been driving me absolutely crazy, so I purchased this Maxforce Quantum Imidacloprid bait and put it down near a couple of places where the ants were gathering.

In a few minutes, the ants showed interest in the bait stations. For the next 24 hours or so, there was much activity at the stations. After 48 hours, the activity had stopped completely. This product worked much faster than TERRO ever did (when TERRO still worked). My sanity is slowly returning, and I’m extremely happy about trying this product.

CAUTIONARY NOTE: Although imidacloprid is widely used (since 1996) as an agricultural insecticide, it may be a factor in the recent bee colony collapse problem. It is highly toxic to bees, and moderately toxic to some birds, plants, and aquatic creatures. (You can Google “imidacloprid” for more information in a Wikipedia article.)

The Maxforce product label warns that this ant bait is toxic to pets and humans if ingested or absorbed through the skin, and that it must NOT be used where it can contaminate food preparation areas. BE SURE TO READ THE LABEL INSTRUCTIONS carefully, and follow the safety precautions.
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text:
In my experience, sprinklers are effective at repelling small animals. I ordered this SCARECROW MOTION ACTIVATED ANIMAL REPELLENT in hopes of discouraging squirrels that eat my succulent plants. However, I can't use it, because people must walk close to my at-risk plantings. The area that I wanted to protect is too small to permit aiming the sprinkler so that it won't spray people as well as squirrels.

The sprinkler itself works as expected. I only wish that the “scarecrow” (big eyes and beak) came as part of the sprinkler head. The sprinkler head is black plastic, and you must apply stickers (included) to make it look like the scarecrow bird in the product page photos.
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text:
My war with ants is one I cannot win, because my home sits atop a gigantic, interconnected ant colony that stretches for miles along the Northern California coastline. However, I can keep their numbers down so that they don't come into the house.

Like all Terro ant baits, this TERRO T901-6 ANT KILLER PLUS (3lb. Shaker Bag) is highly effective against ants. For large outdoor areas, the granules are easier to apply than any of the Terro liquid outdoor ant bait products. The “Plus” part of the name means that the bait kills other insects besides ants. The product is intended to be used as a barrier around your house foundation, along with Terro liquid indoor ant bait stations inside the house as needed.

The bait granules are small, but heavy enough NOT to be picked up by the slightest of breezes (as happens with ant/slug/snail bait in "meal" form). Because they're small, the granules are easily broadcast with the shaker bag. There is no need to touch the bait.
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text:
This ALLSOP HOME & GARDEN 31586 CLEAN-UP CANVAS SUPER DUTY TARP WITH INTERLOCKING HANDLES (300 lb Capacity) is an excellent gather-it-up-and-carry-it tarp, and I like the pretty leaf design that's printed on the outside.

However, it's much harder to use than the advertising suggests. First, I found it difficult to rake old mulch from a flower bed onto the tarp. The rake would lift the tarp edges, and a lot of mulch would slide underneath the tarp. Second, although it was easy to gather the mulch into the center of the tarp (using the handles), it was hard to empty the tarp into a trash container (to be picked up in regular weekly rounds).

There are probably gardeners’ techniques that solve both problems, but the tarp didn't work out for my purposes. I wanted it for leaf cleanup in two small patio areas, and it's suitable mostly for large yards or gardens.
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text:
Ortho is my go-to brand for pest control, and Ortho Home Defense is the product that I use for general insect control. It kills an extremely wide variety of pests, including hard-to-control carpet beetles. However, I use it sparingly indoors, because it makes me sneeze until it has thoroughly dried. This ORTHO HOME DEFENSE MAX INSECT KILLER FOR INDOOR & PERIMETER (0220810, RTU Trigger) is an improvement over the Home Defense aerosol that I've been using. My sense of smell isn't that great, but this product seems to be odor-free, and it dries clear.

Because it's a liquid spray, this Home Defense product goes onto surfaces where I want it, and doesn't blow back as much as the aerosol does. Please note that this sprayer is intended primarily to create a long-lasting barrier against insect entry, and should be applied along foundations, window frames, cabinet bases, and everywhere else that insects enter home and living spaces. It’s not really intended for spraying onto insects directly when there are active infestations.

This container includes a trigger sprayer attachment for use when you have large areas to cover. I added an insect barrier around my condo, and had lots of insecticide left over in the 1 gallon container. The barrier needs to be resprayed every 3 to 12 months.
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text:
This YARD GOODS “CHEEP EATS” FOOD TRUCK BIRD FEEDER is incredibly cute and amusing, but it’s NOT very practical. First, it’s very small—about the size of a small loaf of bread—so it requires frequent refills if you have a lot of birds. Second, the truck is made of unglazed white ceramic, so it may not come clean if bird droppings collect on it (unglazed ceramic is porous).

The food truck feeder is easy to set up—the metal feeder tray goes in crosswise, and there’s a long piece of heavy twine attached for tying the feeder onto a hook or tree limb. Because of the small size, I rate this product at 3 stars ("It's okay" on the official Amazon scale).
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text:
I keep ORTHO FLOWER, FRUIT AND VEGETABLE INSECT KILLER CONCENTRATE on hand to deal with general insect problems that arise in my condo's small patio garden. It controls tomato caterpillars, cucumber beetles, aphids, and whiteflies, as long as I apply it before the problem develops into an infestation. (For infestations on flowers and ornamentals, I use a systemic pesticide that the plant draws up through its roots, but this is NOT an alternative for vegetables, because it makes the entire plant poisonous.) For edible crops, it's important to follow the label instructions about waiting a certain number of days after application before harvesting anything.

This particular product is a concentrate, so you need either a tank sprayer or an Ortho hose-end applicator to apply it. (I like to use a tank sprayer for pesticides, because I'm sure how much pesticide is going onto the plants, when I mix the chemical myself into a known quantity of water.) If you don't want to mix chemicals, you can purchase this same product in pre-mixed spray cannisters and jugs.

Be sure to check the label's list of controlled insects before you buy, because no pesticide is effective against every possible garden pest. You can find the complete label information on the Ortho web site, which even has photos of the controlled insects! Note that this is an insecticide--if you have black spot or other foliage problems, you will need a "garden disease" product that controls plant virus and fungus infections.
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text:
These SCOTT'S EVERY DROP GRANULES really do make a difference in the appearance of my outdoor plants!

In California, outdoor plantings can be watered only twice a week for 15 minutes (because of the current severe drought). I have a small patch of dollhouse fern ground cover and a short privet hedge that have survived with twice-weekly hand-waterings (no deep soakings), but they haven't been looking particularly healthy. After I applied these wetting agent granules, and the same amounts of water that I'd been using, the plants really perked up. The dollhouse fern, in particular, is standing higher, which means it is absorbing more water.

These granules are a wetting agent--not a fertilizer--that helps move water into dried out or compacted soil. My garden has a dreadful clay soil that dries out hard--despite having huge amounts of soil amendment added over many years. This means that water from rain or from a hose will run off the "clay pot" surface until the top layers are moistened. After I applied this Every Drop product, much more of the water seemed to sink into the ground right away around the plants.

This 10 lb. bag covers 5000 sq. ft. The product needs to be reapplied once a month. It seems to be quite safe, as garden products go. It can be applied to ornamental or edible plants. The only bag-label warnings are: "Eye contact may cause irritation. Flush eyes with water as soon as possible after contact".

The granules are also available in a 3.6 lb. [[ASIN:B010872WBE Scotts Every Drop Shaker Jug]], which includes an additional warning: "If applied to edibles, wash before consuming". (The smaller shaker jug can easily be refilled with granules from the larger bag. If you start with the shaker jug, and have a small garden area to cover, you won't need any kind of broadcaster.)

The fact that the product is safe for edibles is important to me, because I grow a few vegetables in containers on my patio. I will be continuing to use this product as long as the California drought restrictions on watering are in place, and probably even after the restrictions are lifted to conserve water generally.

This product is NOT particularly cheap, so you'll want to shop around for the best price. My local hardware/garden store is currently selling the 10-lb. bag for about $12.
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text:
This PREEN GARDEN WEED PREVENTER does NOT kill weeds--it prevents weed seeds from germinating. Thus its effectiveness will depend on how well the soil is prepared before the Preen granules are applied. If the soil contains seeds that have already germinated, those seeds will continue to develop, and will produce weeds. For a casual, though reasonably successful, gardener like myself, there will always be a few weeds that make it through, no matter how hard I work at preparing the garden plot. This product actually did a reasonably good job of preventing weeds from growing in dirt that I added alongside my driveway to fill a channel formed by over-enthusiastic use of an edging tool.

This product is packaged in a large plastic container with a built-in dispenser. This is one of the best-designed dispensers that I've ever encountered, for distributing granules evenly but not too densely across a prepared flowerbed or vegetable plot. Once you apply the Preen, you simply water in the granules. There is an instruction booklet attached to the container that gives detailed instructions for applying the product safely to ornamental plants and to specific types of vegetable crops.

Because I like the packaging of this Preen Garden Weed Preventer, but can't really tell how well it prevents seeds from germinating as compared to alternative products (such as Roundup, which is both a weed killer and a weed preventer), I rate it at 3 stars ("It's okay" on the official Amazon scale).
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text:
This ORTHO BUG B GON INSECT KILLER (HOSE-END SPRAYER) is a good choice for treating large shrubs that develop occasional insect infestations. The blue cypress and arborvitae shrubs alongside my garage have always been susceptible to spider mites, and now they're stressed because of the California drought. When the telltale webbings show up, it's a real chore to spray such large evergreens with little hand-held pump or aerosol cans of insect killer. It always takes several treatments to get full coverage, and to kill all the mites. This hose-end sprayer makes it very easy to apply an even, controlled coating of insect killer to an entire large plant.

The BUG B GON hose-end sprayer attaches easily and tightly (no leaks) to my garden hose. Then I just turn on the water and the sprayer to apply the insect killer. The only tricky parts are remembering (1) to NOT turn the sprayer on until after the water is turned on; and (2) to turn the sprayer off, turn the water off, and then turn the sprayer on partway (to "Water") to clear the hose before disconnecting the sprayer.

Check the label (or the list at the end of this review) to find out whether this product is effective against the insects you are trying to control. (It kills the spider mites that I'm after.) This product is extremely toxic to fish, aquatic invertebrates, oysters, and shrimp, so it shouldn't be used around pet fish or reptiles. The label has different instructions for the application to lawns, ornamental plants, and edible plants (vegetable gardens, fruit and nut trees). The product may need to be reapplied at intervals of 7-14 days or more; and edible vegetables, fruit, and nuts cannot be harvested for 1, 3, 7, 14, or 21 days after application, depending on the specific edible.

This product doesn't appear to be as toxic to humans as many insect killers that I've used. The instructions warn: "Harmful if swallowed. Causes moderate eye irritation. Avoid contact with eyes or clothing. Wear long-sleeved shirt and long pants, socks, shoes, and gloves. Wash thoroughly with soap and water after handling and before eating, drinking, chewing gum, or using tobacco. Prolonged or frequently repeated skin contact may cause allergic reaction in some individuals."

The complete list of controlled insects is as follows:

*Ornamentals: Aphids, armyworms, bagworms, crickets, cutworms, Eastern spruce gall, adelgade, elm leafbeetles, European pine sawfly, fall webworm, flea beetle, grasshoppers, greenbug aphids, gypsy moth, hornets, Japanese beetle (adults), lacebug, leafhoppers, loopers, lygus bugs, mealybugs, mimosa webworms, mosquitoes, northern pine weevil, oak webworm, orchard weevil, periodical cicada, pine chaffer, pine shoot weevil, plantbug, redheaded pine sawfly, saltmarsh caterpillar, sap beetle, sawfly, spittlebug, spruce mites, stinkbug, tarnished plant bug, tent caterpillar, thrips, two-spotted spider mite, wasp, whitefly, and Zimmerman pine moth.

*House foundations: Ants (including carpenter ant, Lasius, Argentine, red harvester, pavement, odorous, pyramid, pharoah), centipedes, cockroaches (including American, Asian, German), crickets, earwigs, fleas, house flies, millipedes, mosquitoes, Palmetto bugs, scorpions, sowbugs, spiders, and ticks (including American, deer tick, brown dog tick).

*Lawns: Ants (including carpenter, lawn, and Argentine), armyworms, black turfgrass ataenius, chinchbugs, crickets, cutworms, deer ticks, fleas, greenbugs (aphids), Japanese beetles (adults), mole crickets, mosquitoes, sod webworms, weevils (including billbugs and annual bluegrass).
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text:
This ORTHO ROSE AND FLOWER INSECT CONTROL PLUS MIRACLE-GRO PLANT FOOD GRANULES is, first and foremost, a systemic pesticide. Systemic pesticides are applied to the soil around a plant, and the plant is watered so that it will take the pesticide in through its roots. This makes the entire plant poisonous to many insects, both good (e.g., ladybugs, bees) and bad (e.g., aphids). Because of the danger to good insects, systemic pesticides are a last resort, to be used when all other methods (e.g., knocking aphids off with a water jet, suffocating scale with neem oil or insecticidal soap, applying surface insect control sprays) have failed.

Systemic insecticides are extremely effective against insect infestations on ornamental plants, and Ortho makes very good insecticides. I've used Ortho systemics with good results on oleander bushes that were dying from scale infestation, and on rose, hibiscus, and sweet pea bushes that were under all-out attack from aphids or whiteflies. Just remember that systemic insecticides should only be used on ornamental plants, because they make the entire plant poisonous. They should NOT be used on plants grown for food (including vines and trees that bear fruit or nuts).

Miracle-Gro plant food for roses and flowering plants has long been my favorite ornamental plant food. It has indeed produced large blooms and healthy foliage on my roses, hibiscus, and brugmansia (Angel's trumpet).

This combination systemic insect control and plant food simplifies the gardener's job by combining two regular tasks into one. The product may be applied every 8 weeks during the growing season. This 4-pound container will treat 32 rose bushes (using a capful, or 2 oz., per bush), or will treat many more container plants. (The "cap" to be used for measuring appears to be the clear plastic cap found on top of the colored screw cap with the shaker top.)

This particular formulation is highly toxic to marine invertebrates, so you should be careful to prevent water runoff to streams and waterways from treated soil. The product is designed to control adelgids, aphids, armored scales (suppression), fungus gnats, Japanese beetle adults, lacebugs, leaf beetles (including Elm, Viburnum, and Lily), leafhoppers (including Glassy-winged Sharpshooter), leafminers (including Birch, Boxwood, and Holly), mealybugs, pine tip moth larvae, psyllids, root weevil larvae (including Black Vine weevils), sawflies (larvae), soft scales (including Lobate lac scales), thrips, white grub larvae (such as Japanese beetles, Oriental beetles, and Asiatic garden beetles), and whiteflies.
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text:
This ORTHO TREE AND SHRUB INSECT CONTROL GRANULES product is a systemic pesticide. Systemic pesticides are applied to the soil around a plant, and the plant is watered so that it will take the pesticide in through its roots. This makes the entire plant poisonous to many insects, both good (e.g., bees, ladybugs) and bad (e.g., aphids, scale, leafminers, whiteflies). Because of the danger to good insects (particularly bees), systemic pesticides are a last resort, to be used when all other methods (e.g., knocking aphids off with a water jet, suffocating scale with neem oil or insecticidal soap, applying surface insect control sprays) have failed.

Systemic insecticides are extremely effective against insect infestations on ornamental plants, and Ortho makes very good insecticides. I've used Ortho systemics with good results on oleander bushes that were dying from scale infestation, and on rose, hibiscus, and sweet pea bushes that were under all-out attack from aphids or whiteflies. Just remember that systemic insecticides should only be used on ornamental plants, because they make the entire plant poisonous. They should NOT be used on plants grown for food (including vines and trees that bear fruit or nuts).

This 3.5-pound container will treat a few trees or several shrubs. You apply 1/3 to 1/2 cup per inch of tree trunk diameter; 1/4 to 1/3 cup per foot of shrub height; or 3/4 to 1 teaspoon for an 8" container plant. This particular formulation is designed to control adelgids, aphids, borers (including the Emerald Ash Borer), fungus gnats, lacebugs, leafhoppers, leaf beetles (including Asian Longhorned Beetles), leafminers, mealybugs, psyllids, root weevils, scales, thrips, whiteflies, and white grubs.
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text:
This TREEGATOR JUNIOR PRO WATERING BAG does a fine job of watering my beloved "Angel's Trumpet" (brugmansia) shrub. It fits nicely around the trunk-like base, and it eliminates the weeds that want to spring up around the base when I water that area with a garden hose.

Because the product is described as "slow release", I was expecting a watering bag that would release the water slowly, as the soil dried out and the plant needed more water. Instead, the watering bag just empties out slowly (in 5-6 hours), regardless of how much water I put in it.

However, the watering bag is easy to fill, and it holds a measured amount (15 gallons). Thus my Angel's Trumpet is now on a schedule of one bag every three days, which keeps it happy and helps me to conserve water. A reminder on my electronic calendar tells me whenever it's time to go out and fill 'er up.

UPDATE June 23, 2016: My TREEGATOR JUNIOR PRO lasted for 2 years before finally springing a leak at the welded seam of the "donut hole". It was left outdoors year round, in temperate California weather averaging 60 degrees F. It experienced some days below freezing, and many hot days in the 80s and 90s. It was generally filled at least once a week. I just ordered a replacement.

text:
This ORIGIN POINT GARD'N BORDER FOLDING FENCE (32" high x 10' long) is a decorative, lightweight metal fence that's easy to set up in your yard or garden. You can use it to surround a flower bed, alongside a pathway (to keep visitors on the straight and narrow), or in many other places. Just don't make my mistake: 32" is the height of the fence, not the length of each folding section.

My fence--just a single section of it--is set up next to the sidewalk that leads to my front door. It prevents an entrance gate from swinging back too far and crushing the flowers in the bed beside the gate. For this purpose, the 32" height is okay, but if I had understood the product description, I would have purchased the 24" high fence instead (see "Origin Point 052410 Gard'n Border Round Folding Fence, White, 24-Inch x 10-Feet").

These folding fences can easily be shortened, using only a flat screwdriver (to pry with) and pliers. They're intended to hook together into a circle, but you can buy two or more units and hook them together in straight lines, squares, or circles. The metal is about as thick and strong as a wire coat hanger, so all you need is a hack saw for removing unwanted connector extensions. The prongs that hold the fence upright go easily into the ground. Just bear in mind that this fence is mostly decorative, and it can easily be bent or knocked over by children or large pets.

text:
Apparently my condo was built on a giant anthill. Whenever it rains, outdoor ants come in. For years, I've controlled them with [[ASIN:B000AYG03C Ant Stakes]] or [[ASIN:B003KVAWZE Diatomaceous Earth]]. Unfortunately, these tried-and-true products are totally ignored by my new indoor ants--itty-bitty creatures that don't carry much bait back to their home nests inside my condo's walls. Happily, my unwanted indoor ants positively love these TERRO PREFILLED LIQUID ANT KILLER II BAITS. It isn't so necessary for them to carry the bait home, when the individuals are lining up for their chance at the bait.

You must exercise some care in using these highly effective liquid baits. To "activate" a bait station, you hold it upright, cut open the plastic along a red line on the underside, and place the station label-side-up in a location where ants are seen. The liquid contents do not spill easily, but the baits should be kept away from children, pets, and food preparation areas. Empty bait stations can be disposed of in household trash, but partly filled stations should be disposed of as hazardous waste (and never poured down indoor or outdoor drains).

text:
We actually got this as a second Pizza Oven so we can host even bigger backyard pizza making parties. Our first pizza oven is the Cuisinart CPO-600 Alfrescamore Portable Outdoor Pizza Oven, which is a piece of beauty that we did not want to take along on camping trips, so we got this one as our "toss in the car and go" oven. And that's what we immediately did, we tossed it in the car and took it to the drive in and used it to make some quick pizzas using Naan bread for a crust and Trader Joes sauces. It was about as easy as you can get, and in big part because of what this oven brings to the table.

Comparison to Cuisinart Pizza Oven

HEAT
The Pizzacraft has the Cuisinart beat hands down. We used the Cuisinart through a Minnesota Winter and when temps were around zero, it took 15 minutes or so to cook a pizza. Even in warmer weather, it never achieved the brick oven temps that this oven achieved easily at the drive in. We were popping out our pizza's in 5-6 minutes consistently and turning the dial down to keep it from 700-800 degrees. This might be because this oven uses a full propane tank while the Cuisinart uses the disposable containers. Also of note, it was completely cooled off by the time the movie was over so we could pack it back into the car.

LOOKS
The Cuisinart is the Cadillac of pizza ovens, it's stainless steel lines are beautiful while the pizza craft is more of a workhorse, function over form oven. This one looks fine, but it is definitely the ugly cousin with strong, hard working limbs.

EASE OF USE
This is another thing that the Pizzacraft excels in. The auto light capabilities makes this far easier to light, while the use of disposable tanks makes the Cuisinart easier to transport. However both make wonderful pizzas which is the end goal. Both are easy to get pizzas into and out of, and both make for a fun picnic.

ASSEMBLY
The Cuisinart edges out the PizzaQue in ease of assembly, although both were fairly easy. It took about 20 minutes to unpack and assemble this oven, versus 10-15 for the Cuisinart. It is easy to attach the canisters to either.

ACCESSORIES
This might be where the PizzaQue falls behind the Cuisinart the most. The Cuisinart came with several accessories, most importantly the pizza peel. That is the one absolutely necessary piece of equipment that you will have to have. Luckily we had an extra that is a little smaller than the one that came with the Cuisinart so we could put and pull pizza into this oven. That fact that it did not come with one is probably the biggest mark against this oven. Both come with the same basic additional accessories otherwise (i.e. stand, expanded utensil kits)

Pros
+ Maintains very hot, brick oven Temps
+ Allows you to make incredible pizzas
+ Auto light option is very nice
+ Portable
+ Seems Rugged.
+ Will take a standard grill propane tank

Cons
- Not quite as beautiful as the Cuisinart
- Does not come with a pizza peel
- Rattles a little in the car during transport
- A little bit of assembly before use

Summary
This is a workhorse oven that may not be as beautiful as the Cuisinart, but the addition of the built in igniter and the ability to maintain, higher pizza crushing temperatures make this as good or better when it comes to functionality. It is solid and easy to use. We loved our first one and loved hosting pizza events for kids and adults alike. It really does make an event in itself and I recommend one of these for anyone who enjoys outdoor dining. The fact that this is $80 cheaper than the Cuisinart is not meaningless. You get a little less in the looks department, but lose nothing (and actually gain a little) in functionality.

I am contacting the company (800-323-5800) to see how we can preserve the looks here where everything including stainless steel will rust given enough time, we may end up bringing it in the garage when not in use. It really is a gem and makes for a wonderful evening treat to invite friends over and socialize.

*Approved items for burning: wood or self-starting fire logs or charcoal.

NOTE: Finish will not look the same after burning. Color change to the metal on the outside of your pit is normal, and should be expected. The color change occurs due to the effects of weather, heat, and smoke. To limit this color change, burn clean high-quality wood and keep your fire pit covered when not in use. You can also clean after each use with soap, water, and the metal-appropriate polish and touch paint up with a high-temperature paint if so desired.

NOTE: Add at least 1 inch of sand to the bottom of the fire tub. This will distribute the heat more evenly.

*Parts
A. Woven Base (1)
B. Cast Iron Fire Bowl (1)
C. Leg (3)
D. Grate (1)
E. M6 x 1 Screw (7)
F. Spark Guard (1)
G. Washer (1)
H. Spark Guard Handle (1)
I. Vinyl Cover (1)
J. Spark Guard Tool(1)

1. Attach LEGS (C) to WOVEN BASE (A) using SCREWS (E)
2. Attach HANDLE (H) to SPARK GUARD (F) using SCREW (E) and WASHER (G)
3. Place FIRE BOWL (B) inside BASE (A).
4. Place GRATE (D) inside FIRE BOWL (B).

text:
and customer service is beyond excellent. We have a small front yard fountain in Oregon, on the coast.

Our issue is not so much sludge as it is sediment from things falling in and cloudy water. A cup of this once a week in our 100 gallon 'pond' has made maintenance easy and the water clear to the bottom. Once a month now I skim the bottom (Black plastic resin). I am thinking of adding rocks and then just cleaning once or twice a year.

From the manufacturer, Great Lakes Bio Systems to a user with different issues: It looks like your biggest issue might be water clarity (more so than sludge/muck). Reducing the sludge layer will ultimately help your water clarity but it's not the fastest way to go, you will likely have better luck with Orb-3 Lake and Pond Enzymes Jug, 1-Gallon. Please drop me a message at info at greatlakesbio dot com with any questions.

And for those with Ponds: Pond turnover is normal, it generally occurs in the spring & fall. Pond water tends to create "layers" based on the water temperature. As the water temperature changes with the seasons, the cooler water sinks to the bottom & the warmer water rises to the top. This inversion carries whatever happens to be in that layer with it, often resulting in visible changes to the water clarity.

There are a lot of moving parts to a water body, just like the human body. Once it falls out of balance it can be difficult to get it back on track. In a balanced water body, the wastes produced by fish & other wildlife are consumed by plants & beneficial bacteria. If large volumes of nutrients are suddenly introduced into the water (such as by the runoff you described from the road construction), plants & bacteria will grow their population & attempt to consume them. Likely it is some type of plant life that is causing the brown water.

Ideally you would be able to grow the bacteria population so that it outcompetes the plants. Unfortunately the bacteria population is often limited by other factors. For example, often times there is a shortage of oxygen in the water, which will affect the bacterial population much more than the plant population - causing an explosion of algae, weeds, etc. Trace Mineral content can also be lacking. In the human analogy, imagine living in an environment where there is tons of good food but very little air. The human population would be unable to thrive & grow due to the lack of air, despite all of the good food.

The best universal recommendation I can offer is to get plenty of aeration in the water. A good quality aeration system will supply oxygen to the bacteria and also provide continuous mixing of the water. The extra oxygen will support increased bacterial populations which will begin to consume the additional organics and attempt to out-compete the weeds.

If you want to kick-start the process you can introduce bacteria & enzymes. For example, Orb-3 Lake and Pond Liquid Bacteria Jug, 1-Gallon contains strains of bacteria that are known to be voracious consumers of waste products found in ponds, and are likely more aggressive than the native bacteria. Treating with enzymes, such as Orb-3 Lake and Pond Enzymes Jug, 1-Gallon, will break down the organics in the water, making them quicker/easier for the bacteria to digest - like converting apples to apple sauce.

Reducing the black muck/sludge layer at the bottom of the pond may help but Orb-3 Sludge Pellets will work much better in situations where the water column itself is healthy & in balance & not overloaded with nutrients or lacking oxygen.

Getting a pond back into natural balance can take a great deal of time - depending on how bad the situation is it can require multiple seasons of active management & treatment with natural products before you can switch back to maintenance mode.

If you're looking for a great source of local expertise, give the IPPCA a shot. You can find them at IPPCA dot com - coincidentally they are located in GA as well.

text:
[[VIDEOID:5d5875bf79e0f75906b36f3d740c6881]] I was skeptical. But they truly work. Easy to use. Cut hole in the top of the bag. Pull up the “plug” topper. Add string, hook etc to hang it by. Add water and wait for the flies to meet their demise. Our garage was filled with house flys this year. Crazy. My stick tape wasn’t cutting it. Decided to buy these and give it a try. I’m thrilled I did! I originally hung it right inside my big garage door. That got them started. But after the first day, the whiff of dead body was too much. Ha! Seriously, it smells awful. Initially it was like a faint moth ball. But after the water dissolved the special mix, it was game on. Yuck. I’m thinking they use dehydrated roadkill from July.
I easily moved it outside the garage door and hung it from our side lights and near our big trash can. I’ll attach 2 photos. Plus a short video. One pic from the night and one the next morning. You can clearly see the difference in the amount of dead flies and others begging to jump in. I’ve never seen anything like it. We had a big rain storm and it survived fine. Blew up yard debris on my siding. But, the body bag was still collecting its victims. I think I captured the neighborhood army of flies. Honestly, best fly trap ever.
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text:
I chose this metal detecting pinpointer to assist with metal detecting, to make it easier to locate an unearthed object once it has been dug out. So far it has worked out well, though I think it probably could be better.

This is a small device, not weighing much even when the battery is installed and small enough to fit in my jacket pocket. It comes with a belt clip as well as a lanyard so it is easy to handle and keep track of it when carrying. It comes with a 9V battery.

In use this works fairly well and it is able to pinpoint items without too much effort. It doesn't seem to work at depths of more than a few inches but that's not the intent of this device. When it detects the presence of metal is makes a beep that is distinct and is loud enough to be heard but not deafening. It can be configured to vibrate as well, or even beep and vibrate together.

I conducted a few tests and made a discovery with this device. It easily detects gold, coins and other common metals as expected. However, when I used it to scan my glasses, which have a titanium frame, the device never made a sound as if it had no clue there was metal close by. I don't know how often I would encounter titanium in "the wild " but it is a metal and I would expect this scanner to pick it up. I have a tungsten ring against which I need to try this as well - I suspect that will be a much more common test case.

This is a decent wand scanner, with some limitations. It works for most common metals, but it doesn't work on everything. I will be working with it more and will update this review as I learn.
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text:
I selected this trimmer for working in my yard specifically; I have a small yard and I wanted a light-duty trimmer that can handle the whole yard without too much effort or trouble. It has met my expectations and it works well for me. I do have one complaint with it as delivered - it is pre-wound with cheap string.

This unit comes packed up nicely and requires a little bit of assembly. It took me maybe 10 minutes from start to finish before it was ready to go. With the battery in place it feels balanced and is easy to handle.

The battery was dead as a doornail when delivered and I needed to charge it up before this trimmer could be used at all. That was a minor annoyance but not a big deal. Many tools and appliances like this have at least some charge in the battery, so this was a minor disappointment that it wasn't usable immediately. However, it gave me a chance to charge the battery from dead to full and it took under an hour.

The trimmer worked as expected and I was able to trim the edges around my property on a single battery charge with power to spare. That's all I can ask. I don't know if this would last for a large piece of property, though based on battery remaining I think it would would conk out before completing a large (1 acre or larger) edge job, though that it speculation.

The string on this trimmer is the cheap gray stuff that wears quickly. It is the main issue I have with it, though it will eventually resolve itself and will all be gone. I have good string that I use on my other trimmer that I will spool onto this one when the current spool runs out (which shouldn't take too long).

Other than being wound with cheap, soft string, I like this trimmer and it meets my needs. It works well and is decently balanced. The jury is still out regarding battery endurance but so far it is sufficient to do my yard.
product: Ortho GroundClear Year Long Vegetation Killer

text:
[[VIDEOID:c87e962bc893a948856b0f1b285ce6cc]] I wanted to love this bc I previously bought a matching turquoise teapot, but the loose lid (defective or design flaw? Idk) on the cups is driving me batty. I’m disabled so my gait is not great to begin with & the lid just bangs non-stop while I walk from my kitchen to wherever I’m going with my tea. It’s incredibly annoying. I had hoped it was just a one-off so I purchased it in another color & sadly it has the same exact problem. They could fix the problem by adding a rubber gasket or flange to the lid imo & I even thought of doing so myself until I accidentally knocked the cup over due to a design flaw that has a small base on the cup. I like the lid bc I run a fan continuously & I live with 2 service dogs so I like to keep my drinks covered beyond just the brew times so I really hope they update this cup bc it does keep the tea warm & the size is perfect for a 2 cup brew.

I wish they would fix the obvious design flaw of the base that is too small for a cup that tall & wide bc it is also very easy to accidentally knock over as I mentioned above. I did it several times before I yet boxed them both back up.

Overall I like the product as it seems as it was made with quality in mind, but missed the mark in the design phase. The colors are pretty & the cups I purchased keep the tea warm, but the lids are either defective or a design flaw. I really hope the company fixes these issue bc the colors match teapots I already own. Lol. I intend to see if the company has another design that works better for me in the meantime.
product: YETI Rambler 20 oz Tumbler

text: If you live at a higher elevation like me (5k Colorado) just know that after the buzzer beeps you might wanna leave the hard boiled eggs inside the steamed cooker for another minute or two if you want the egg cooked all the way through. I also add a bit more water than the fill line says, but again that’s bc of my elevation & it evaporates quickly.  It’s not any quicker than I used to make them, but it’s a lot less water. I’m disabled & have a TBI rn so the auto-cutoff was a no-brained for me. I bought the blue bc it was cheapest.  My service 🐶  🐶 love hard boiled eggs too so we are enjoying this new addition to our kitchen.  My only regret is that I did not buy it sooner & that Amazon never has the color I want for the cheapest price, but I can live with the bb blue.  We have used it 3x & so far it’s working okay. Oh- don’t place it under upper cabinets as it has a vent hole & it will vent & it will make the cabinets sweat.  Give it some room to breathe imo.  I keep a pair of tongs handy to unload the eggs & dunk them in my ice bath. Yes. You end up using water again, but I have house plants I’ll use it on anyways so it not just wasted water.  If it holds up & doesn’t break down it will truly be one of my top 10 if not top 5 amazon lifetime purchases.  It’s that good.  Nothing is worse than watching eggs boil. Lol.  I start this up at the same time I start my tea brewing and go about feeding the wildlife in my backyard.  It’s just a very peaceful start to my day. I know ppl have said stuff about the buzzer, but honestly I happen to be hearing impaired in both ears & my service dogs are trained to hear for me. I like that it’s loud enough that if my pups are outside & I am in my living room or just outside on my patio with them & my screen door is open then they & often I can still hear it.  I wish appliances had volume options on their alarms, but until then I am very happy with this one.  I am just extremely happy with this purchase. It’s been a long time since I could say that. Lol.
product: Zwilling J.A. Henckels Pro 8-Inch Chef's Knife

text:
When I got this I immediately tested it and made a salad to store in the fridge overnight, then shook the container a bit to test how well it separated things and ate it the next day. Nothing leaked and the plate kept the greens and the toppings from mixing so nothing got soggy. It was also easy to clean. The build seems very sturdy and high quality, I would buy this again or recommend it. The silicone gasket is easy to remove, clean, and replace. The included dressing cup is a good size and did not leak, but does not have a gasket, just a cap that screws on tightly. Because the materials in this container are on the thicker side and seem very sturdy, I don't anticipate any issues with leaks.

It's probably good for storing pasta dishes as well but it's especially good for salads because it keeps the ingredients separate. I was able to fit about 3 cups (60g) of greens which is enough for a fairly large salad! I think the manufacturer did a good job of making this large enough to fit a large, filling dish but not so large that it's hard to store or transport. I bought this to take to my office for lunches.

text: This is a great hand mixer. I actually have only used mixers before for cake batters and whipping cream purposes. But with this B&D Performance Premium hand mixer, I find I can do much more.
