text:
Unfortunately the wireless network drivers for this laptop did not work out do the box. I'd be able to connect to router only for it to crash a few seconds later, rebooted over and over with the same thing happening. There is no eithernet plugin for this laptop as it is very thin. I think they still could of added one but maybe they were trying to cut cost and weight. I have a USB to eithernet so I was able to use that and the serial number on the back to access the latest wireless drivers on the dell site. After updating and rebooting I had no more issues with the wireless network card. If you don't have a USB to eithernet adapter you'll need a USB flash drive and another computer to get the updated drivers to this system.

After the hour of troubleshooting and fixing the issue I was able to finally test the system. I had no issues installing all my core software apps MS office, Adobe PS CS5, Adobe Acrobate, and other misc apps. Hard drive seems to be a bit small but everything has gone cloud these days so I don't really see a need for a larger hard drive. Graphics are quite good. As good if not better than my work laptops that cost much more. Touch screen is accurate but I really don't think I could ever use this as a tablet as I'm not very impressed with MS OS for tablet use. Over all a solid 4 start laptop (5 if I hadn't had the wireless NIC issues) if you can find for $450. Currently selling for $499. And I think that is just a bit hig. A very nice light option that will work nicely for most laptop users.
product: Apple AirTag 4 Pack

text:
PCMARK score was 3148. Fastest PC laptop I have ever benchmarked.

Pros:
1. Display looks amazing. Resolution is comparable to my Iphone, which is very sharp.
2. Networking rang works better than any device I have tested. Router is on the 2nd floor and I can take this into my garage, sit in my car and still get a decent signal. Using the 5Ghz I can stream MKV movie files that are over 30GB's.
3. Processor. I haven't been able to throw anything extreme at the Intel Core i7-4710HQ but it should be more than adequate for my needs. Post processing videos and photo editing.
4. Graphics card. NVIDIA GeForce GTX 860M. I installed Audocad on this system and this card actually performs as good if not better than my Quadro 2000 that's in my workstation.
5. Keyboard has red back lighting. Looks really awesome.
6. Battery lasts about 4 to 5 hours with not stop use.
7. Comes with DVD/BluRay player.

Cons
1.Onboard camera could do better in low light. Not a big deal for me.

I will report back with more info after I use it some more. I only had it for a week.

Great laptop. Great value.
product: Apple AirTag 4 Pack

text: Easy to setup. Comes with software to walk you through setup and configuration within 5 min. UI is basic and would be easy for a novice to configure advance settings. I would recommend this model over higher end model/vendors to users that want something easy and quick. If you need more advanced control and security it might not be the best fit. Strong WiFi signal, iPhone's work at our pool which is probably 80 to 100 yards from router/home. Devices tested with were 2 laptops a workstation and some iPhone's.

text: These are regular mica powders!  One appears to have had some interference added, but the others are devoid of the addition.  They have literally just mixed 3 colors together so instead of getting the cute colors they advertise in their photos you get an ugly brown from the purple/red-blue/green, a gold from the blue/green/gold & a bronze from the yellow/orange/bronze.  Only one of my envelopes even appeared to have the added micas. Two envelopes appeared to only have the hold mica and bronze mica without the additional two colors they were supposed to have as if they decided to just not even bother adding them.  I purchased these to make color shift watercolor paints & as such they are utterly useless. There is no way in the world that you can possibly get the results that they advertise in they’re pictures from the envelopes they mail you. It is physically & chemically impossible!  I am disgusted.  $8 for 15 grams of useless product & worse it’s the exact same mica powders “Riya” that I purchased in a set of 100 last week & had already tried to mix that way for a color shift which was why I was specifically looking for color shift mica!  These are metallic. They are in no way color shift!  Now I’ll have to see what the return/refund policy is with amazon as how do you return something you have made & put into ur own paint palettes before you found out they were garbage? Hmm?
product: Crayola Inspiration Art Case

text:
[[VIDEOID:8644a01103c90ab8f83af816b83881be]] Likes: the colors are fabulous. I love that the stamp pads can be stacked! I like that I can run my stamps over the pad & not just press it down inside a stamp pad. Yes, it messes up the coloring, but I can use it on larger stamps.
Dislikes: the pads are very thin & dried out very fast while literally just sitting in my craft room & not even being used which is seriously annoying me right now. I’m disabled & I live on a fixed income so purchasing products that are consumable & have to be replaced drives me nuts to begin with, but then purchasing a consumable item that has a shelf life of used twice & now useless is beyond frustrating. If I had realized before I would have returned it for a replacement obviously bc even tho I now know it’s not got longevity on its side I would probably still purchase it & just be ready to go on a stamping marathon. Lol. As I have adhd & usually need little sleep it’s always a possibility lol.

Unfortunately I was in an accident on 2/4 so I only got to try these a couple of times (literally 2x!!! Woe is me!). & was bedridden & unable to do anything after that until today 3/24 when I opened these again & decided to try to carve a stamp of my Pekingese /Japanese Chin/ Shih Tzu mix puppy Sophie Wynter. Btw: the stamp carving kit is the speedball one sold here on Amazon for $15. I also bought the one by Pixiss for around $10 I think and honestly both are worth the $ imo. I live in chronic pain & I am always trying to find things that will help get my mind off the horrors of living with it day in & day out. It doesn’t make the pain go away, but it does give me something to remind myself that I survived another day with the help of my service pups.
The Stamps: great out of the pkg. they were moist & juicy, but had no staying power. If you have kids that will use them fast or if you intend to use them fast then I think you will like them. However, if you are looking for something to add to your craft stores for ongoing project & future projects & would want to go back & add to stuff later then you need to keep looking or just be prepared to buy more.
product: Crayola Inspiration Art Case

text:
Lowered from a 3 star review to a 1 star bc the very obviously defective stamps were replaced with another set of very obviously defective stamps! It drives me nuts to waste my time, $, & energy to purchase something, wait on it to arrive, then go through the hassle of the returns to then wait for a replacement only to have the replacement be just as defective or often worse than the original. Apparently the entire tim of these stamps was bad & nobody ever bothered to fix it. Ok fine if you wanna sell DEFECTIVE stamps, but be HONEST about it so ppl don’t waste their time, $, & energy on DEFECTIVE items they would not have chosen to begin with. I am sure some ppl would still purchase the set. I might even at the right price point, but not like this. Not believing I’m buying something in good condition & not a DEFECTIVE item which both of these are.
OLD REVIEW:
Some of these are pretty nice, but the cardinal & starling are a hot mess. The pigeon is flawed too, but works. Idk if their designer messed up or their manufacturer did, but the actual bird designs are not true to life on several either which I find to be most annoying as a bird lover. It would make an ok gift for kids if you can find them on sale & know 1/4 are useless imo. I have no idea why they bothered to put the bird names on the stamps bc you cannot read them. Great in theory, but lousy execution. I would definitely buy them again if they fixed the flaws & I am actually hoping that Amazon will replace them as these are flawed & I am just stupid enough, foolish enough, naive enough, or hopeful enough that the replacement will arrive perfect. Lol! Yes ppl- I live in my own little make believe world. It’s downright magical & days like today I truly prefer my imagination world where anything & everything is possible & Covid doesn’t exist & ppl don’t hate each other for their politics & ppl believe in peace & abhor war to the real one happening outside my door right now. Bottom line: some birds good, some birds very bad, do yourself a favor & cut the names of the birds off the stamps if you choose to buy them & keep them. It’s just a little minor surgery between friends & nobody gets hurt. Besides: doesn’t everyone feel like wielding a sharp instrument every now & then? Especially when you know nobody is going to get hurt? Lol. Safety warning: buy the safety gloves amazon sells so you don’t knick ur own fingers or just start by being smart & using a tweezers or cuticle stick to hold down the stamps while you remove the bird names. Me, myself, & I am too old to start learning how to live without appendages so I always make sure something else is between me, myself, & I & anything Me, myself, & I am cutting. Yes- I am totes all up in my feelings today. All full of myself hence the me, myself, & I. Lol. Happy crafting shoppers! Now get off ur devices & make something! This is a throw down: I hereby challenge you to create a bird journal arts & crafts page today. That’s it! Clock is ticking! Get a move on! Now! Now! Now! Why yes! I was in the Navy! What gave me, myself & I away? Lol. Peace & Love ppl. Live ur beautiful lives!
product: Crayola Inspiration Art Case

text: 🤬I am so tired of receiving items that are so obviously damaged & never should have been packaged or sent in the first place.  Apparently this was not sold by Amazon, but sold by an Amazon third party. Idc anymore. I’m still sick & tired of it. I feel like Amazon needs to do more due diligence to make sure that obviously damaged items & crap & returned items are not being sold on here. Bc you can clearly see both fingerprints on these pics & an obvious burn mark.  Sure, the laser burning the stencil could have made the burn marks. It also could have been left by someone doing pyrography.  Check out the pic of the packaging.  The black sheet that came behind the stencil. I get those from Joann’s or Michael’s 99% of the time they have not a mark on them.  Lately tho anything from Amazon or any of their parties & it looks like the shipping truck has been involved in an accident or it’s come from a war zone.  I swear these third party companies are buying stuff that is seconds or returns or literally insurance write-offs from trucking accidents & selling it as new.  I had sworn off buying anything from third party companies for a long time & foolishly decided to give it a try again. And this is what I got. Lesson learned.  I know there are some good ones out there & I have been fortunate enough to purchase from them & maybe this company was just having a bad day, but I am tired of being burned. It makes it worse that this original order was never delivered to my address as the amazon delivery drivers keep delivering my stuff even after 3 years of living in the same place & literally having 6” numerals of my address right next to my front door to the wrong address. So they write this up as a “replacement”. And they write it up as tho I “returned” the first set which I obviously did not as I never received it!!  38 months now of God knows what ppl receiving my service dogs cookies, food, leashes, harnesses, etc & receiving my art supplies etc.  And it usually starts with a csr saying : well you need to wait a couple hours bc sometimes it says it’s delivered & it’s not yet. Lol. Hello! There’s a picture of a door that is not my door!  Or the other version:  can you go look for it? Lol No! I am a service connected disabled veteran & I pay for amazon prime exactly  for the delivery exactly so that I don’t have to “go look for it” with 18” of snow outside my door & living in an “open carry firearms” state!  Ppl do not like you snooping around their homes with ur phine trying to match amazon pictures of front doors to theirs. Trust me bc I stupidly tried it a few times. And luckily lived to type about it. Barely.  So no Amazon. I will no longer be doing your delivery drivers’ jobs for them.  And if it sounds like I am ticked off it’s bc I am. They have some internal issue where even when I notified them that my service dogs were missing 2 bags of treats & there was not even a picture of the delivery to even remotely guess where they might have been mistakenly delivered they were then unable to replace them!  They had to wait 24 hours to reorder them. This my service dogs did not get the missing dog treats for 4 days!!!  Bc they had issues on their end. So much for next day delivery. Lol. That’s it. That’s my rant. Yes. I feel better. No. I’m not keeping the nasty stencil. It’s going back. My best guess is the blade needed to be changed when they were cutting the stencils & it must have been loose bc you can see extra cut lines all over the top of the stencil from multiple passes. Something was off at the factory.
product: Crayola Inspiration Art Case

I keep finding my dog Sophie’s toys on top of my knitting stuff. I’m going to have to buy her her own bag! Lol.  I love her, but I drew the line at pup saliva & pup hair all over my stitching & yarn.

text: I wanted to love these bc my daughter has the exact same kind. I purchased them on her advice.  Sadly, I did not check them over until today when I needed them. Quite a few of them have the metal shank and hook off he 20-30 degrees. They are left or right facing as opposed to straight up in the plastic as they should be.  This might not be an issue for most ppl, but I’ve had surgery on my crocheting hand and it simply does not work for me. Whole tye handkes are very comfortable they also pick up dog hair, yarn fibers, and any dust floating around your environment. text:
UPDATE BELOW...I have been buying and making diamond painting craft projects for about 1 1/2 years. It's relaxing and satisfying as craft projects go, in my personal opinion. This particular kit is nice for a couple of reasons.
1. The drills (the plastic diamond pieces) are exceptionally gem like. All the colors are sparkly and bright. I've found some kits are dull and therefore look like less quality to me. I prefer the more gem like kits as a personal choice.
2. There VERY few drills that are stuck together. With some kits I've found that most or all the drills are stuck together, which leads to frustration and takes additional time to prep to do the project. Further, sometimes the drills have been very poorly made and random in sizing. In this kit I've found the drills to be fairly uniform with only a few that were unusable due to flaws and sizing.
In order to be successful in diamond painting I do recommend a good light board. Also, remember that the smaller the print, the less detail and more 'pixalation' there is. This kit is about the smallest I choose because I find the detail to be clear. You can purchase kits with round drills or square drills. I like either - I think I prefer square drills for larger projects - but it is tricky to line them up properly - so I believe round drills are better for beginners. Also be aware that some projects are not 'full drill' ... that means that only part of the picture is diamond painted. They are faster to complete, but, in my opinion a bit less dimensional.
All in all I believe this kit to be one of my better purchases. I have just begun the project, so if there are issues (such as not enough drills - which happens sometimes and is very frustrating *a further note: save all left over drills from other projects - on occasion you need a couple extras for subsequent projects) I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful. I hope this review was helpful to you!
UPDATE 11/14/2019: an update already!! I am short of drills! Grrrrr! Contacted seller right away. Was promised another set to rob of drills needed. They say it will be sent 3-5 days. Honestly about 2 out 20 project are short of drills so this doesnt happen very often. Even tho - its frustrating. I should probably drop to 3 stars but I still really appreciated the quality of the drills and project overall. It would be nice if you could just buy more of the drill color you are short - but I have not found an outlet for that. I always save spare drills from other projects however none would work in this case. I'm literally short about 25 drills. Another thing you'll notice if you do enough projects is that while the 'universal' color code may say #3370 - every manufacturer will have a slightly different shade of that #. I've found this when 'filing' my spare drills. I will update further if it seems useful.
product: Crayola Inspiration Art Case

text:
There are 16 rolls of ribbon.. approximately all the same amount (I figured around 24 or so yards each color). They are rolled onto thin, styrofoam like spools with pencil sized holes through them so it you have a pencil sized rod to thread them onto, could unroll the ribbon like a pro. The ribbon itself is pretty thin... it's not super quality.. BUT the price is fair in my opinion. I use the ribbon to wrap around candy bars I bring onto the plane for the crew when I travel. So... it doesn't have to be super sturdy. The colors are bright and cheery. The first one I un-taped at the edge, unraveled.. so carefully remove the tape stoppers or you won't have any ribbon! :) All in all I am happy with the purchase - having said that, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful and I hope this info was helpful to you.
UPDATE 9/2020 - my second purchase....NOTE: The ribbon is still rolled onto a Styrofoam core that has a hole in the center - about the circumference of a pencil ... It is really important - for ease of use- that you keep it on the Styrofoam core. If you accidentally have the ribbon fall off the core - it's a mess that you will end up rewinding. I'm thinking about getting a then dowel to thread the rolls onto. My husband is handy. Perhaps he could make me a wood frame to set the dowel on to roll it off of. Seems like a lot - but I like less frustration!

text: Have used Testors Plastic Cement for decades and it has always been my choice for assembling plastic models.  It is sticky enough to adhere well and dries well and makes a good bond.  It doesn't dry too fast, so you have a little time to move the parts around slightly to get a good fit, before you have a permanent bond.  There are glues out there that are more liquid, but was always afraid to use them for fear of them running too much and ruining the features on the plastic.  Note that this glue (and most plastic glues) works by dissolving a little of the plastic, so you do have to be careful not to use too much. If it oozes too much out of the joints you are gluing, it can damage the features of the plastic.  The only problem I have had with this glue is that you have to be careful to squeeze the tubes evenly or they can break through; they are made of a soft metal that tends to puncture if you aren't paying attention to how the tube gets crimped as you use up the glue.  Testors also makes a good line of paints for models.
product: Crayola Inspiration Art Case

text: Very fast and easy assembly.  It does need some painting to look realistic, since it is all molded in a single color (white) or actually two if you count the clear window glass material.  I used red paint for window frames and 4 of the gas pumps, white for two of the other pumps, silver for rain downspouts and vent on the roof and aged concrete for the pump islands.  Instruction sheet is easy to follow with parts numbered.  I have not quite finished building yet, as one thing is holding me up.  In the pictures on the box, it shows several thin black lines on the trim and I don't know how to get those put on without it oozing over into the white.  Even a very thin brush might not work; maybe a toothpick?  The kit comes with decals and I AWAYS have trouble with decals and usually end up destroying some of them.  What I do as a precaution is to make a couple color photocopies of the decals and then I can always cut out the pieces and glue them on in lieu of the decals. If you really want to do your decals well, you also need to buy a bottle of Micro Sol, which softens the decal and makes them adhere better. (I have it, use it and it works well.)  The kit comes with 3 different sets of 6 gas pumps, so you can use the pumps that fit the era you are modeling.  Unfortunately there are only two pump islands, so you can't swap pumps if you want to change the era, even though you have the pumps for the 3 eras.  (Too bad the kit didn't include three sets of pump islands.)  The only other thing that defines the era is the standing sign; two signs included; one to accommodate Standard oil and the other to accommodate either "Al's Victory Service" or "Jerry's Gas and Garage" on the sign.  The only thing that I really did not like about this kit is that the gas pumps seem to lack detail.  They don't look like they have any dispenser hoses on them; that can make a big difference in realism.  The decals are a bit "congested" on the decal sheet, so be careful.  Also be careful with the plastic sprues that the parts are attached to.  There are a few very tiny parts that can come loose; I almost lost the air pump when it fell off.  This is a decent gas station kit and probably a good kit for a beginner, but I do think there are better looking gas station kits out there.  Generally the Walthers kits are nice, but need a lot of painting to look realistic.  This kit needs less painting than some other kits.  Note: there are quite a few small pieces, so you need patience.  Also this kit and all the garage and gas station kits I have bought and assembled do not come with a base or concrete driveways, so you have to create your own; I used heavy card stock and gray spray paint.  The concrete joints can be made with a lead pencil and a ruler and look quite realistic.
product: Crayola Inspiration Art Case

text: Easy to assemble and easy to use.  Quiet and works well.  You don't need a computer to use it; you can set it up and use it right from the display panel.  There are a limited number of patterns available, so it would be nice if there were more included; however, there are enough for a beginner and you can always get more or presumably scan your own with the machine.  Haven't used the scanning portion yet.  Brother makes good equipment, so I anticipate that this will work for a long time without any trouble.  A great cutting machine for someone who likes crafts and has the need to do a lot of cutting.  Very pleased with it.
product: Crayola Inspiration Art Case

text: The Windmill was a bit of a challenge to assemble, but the main problem is that not many of the parts were numbered.  Many of the parts were just loose, rather than hooked to a plastic strip with a number next to them, as some were.  Also the picture on the box was actually reversed from what the model turned out to be.  I think they printed it backwards. (This one might be the correct way.) The result doesn't look quite as good as the picture.  It would require some painting.  In spite of the shortcomings, I loved the result. Even without anything painted, there are enough colors to make this look very nice.  This is one of the nicest scale model buildings you are going to come across.  The added extras of the lady hanging up the wash and two cows included really makes it come to life.  They put a lot of detail into this model and it really shows.  Of the dozen or so scale model buildings that I have, I like this the best.  If you think you might want it, get it now, as I was told that some of the Life-Like Trains buildings are disappearing and may never return to market. They are the quite economically priced, compared to other scale model buildings, which can get very expensive.  They also have a very realistic look.  You won't be disappointed.  Just be sure you have patience to assemble the kit.  A younger child might find it too difficult.  There are a lot of tiny parts to glue together.  Be careful not to lose any!  I lost one small one for a while, but finally located it.  Requires modeling cement (not included) and some pieces of thread to make the clothes lines (also not included).  A really fun model.
product: Crayola Inspiration Art Case

The variety of colors is nice. There are even some tye-dyed colored bands.  My daughter has a small box of loom bands & supplies. The bands often get mixed up & her box didn't hold all of the supplies. This organizer pretty much holds everything! The box is much larger than I was expecting by at least a couple of inches. I can't wait for my daughter to open this!
